###  Øvrige utgivelser 
Bjørnsons verker foreligger på de fleste kulturspråk. På norsk er det kommet fire samlede utgaver.
  * _Artikler og taler_ (2 bind, 1912–1913) med innledning av J. E. Sars om Bjørnsons plass i Norges politiske historie
  * _Aulestadbreve til fru Bergliot Ibsen_ (1911)
  * brevsamlingene _Grotid_ (2 bind, 1921)
  * _Brytningsår_ (2 bind, 1921)
  * _Kampliv_ (2 bind, 1932) alle utgitt ved [Halvdan Koht](https://snl.no/Halvdan_Koht)
  * _Breve til Alex. L. Kielland_ (1930)
  * _Samlede digte_ (1926)
  * _Tyrolersangen_ (1932), alle ved [Francis Bull](https://snl.no/Francis_Bull)
  * _B. B.s og Chr. Collins brevveksling 1889–1909_ (1937)
  * _B. B.s brevveksling med danske_ (6 bind, 1953–1974) ved [Øyvind Anker](https://snl.no/%C3%98yvind_Anker)
  * Francis Bull og [Torben Nielsen](https://snl.no/Torben_Nielsen).
  * Bjørnsons brevveksling med G. Gran i tidsskriftet Edda (bind 35, årgang 22)
  * Bjørnsons brev til datteren Dagny, _Din venn far_ (1956)
  * _Breve til Karoline_ (1957)
  * _B. B.s brevveksling med svenske 1858–1909_ (3 bind, 1960–1961) _B. B.s Briefwechsel mit Deutscher_ (2 bind, 1986–1987)
  * _God morgen, Rosalinde! Brev til Rosalinde Thomsen_ (1990)
  * dessuten en stor samling brev i L.C. Nielsen: _F.V. Hegel; et mindeskrift_ (2 bind)
  * _Georg og Edv. Brandes' brevveksling_ (1939)

metadata.description: Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson var en av Norges og Europas viktigste forfattere i andre halvdel av 1800-tallet. Hans produksjon er svært omfattende og inkluderer romaner, noveller, teaterstykker og lyrikk. I tillegg til dette var han også journalist, debattant, teatersjef og politisk aktivist.

**Bjørnstjerne Martinius Bjørnson** ([/ˈbjɜːrnsən/](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [_BYURN -sən_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"),[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-1) Norwegian: [[ˈbjø̂ːɳstjæːɳə](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA/Norwegian "Help:IPA/Norwegian"); 8 December 1832 – 26 April 1910) was a Norwegian writer who received the [1903 Nobel Prize in Literature](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1903_Nobel_Prize_in_Literature "1903 Nobel Prize in Literature") "as a tribute to his noble, magnificent and versatile poetry, which has always been distinguished by both the freshness of its inspiration and the rare purity of its spirit". The first Norwegian Nobel laureate, he was a prolific polemicist and extremely influential in Norwegian public life and Scandinavian cultural debate.[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-2) Bjørnson is considered to be one of ["the four greats" of Norwegian literature](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Four_Greats_\(Norwegian_writers\) "The Four Greats \(Norwegian writers\)"), alongside [Ibsen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henrik_Ibsen "Henrik Ibsen"), [Lie](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jonas_Lie_\(writer\) "Jonas Lie \(writer\)"), and [Kielland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Kielland "Alexander Kielland").[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-Groendahl-3) He is also celebrated for his lyrics to the Norwegian national anthem, "[Ja, vi elsker dette landet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ja,_vi_elsker_dette_landet "Ja, vi elsker dette landet")".[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-SNL-4) The composer [Fredrikke Waaler](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fredrikke_Waaler "Fredrikke Waaler") based a composition for voice and piano (_Spinnersken_) on a text by Bjørnson, as did [Anna Teichmüller](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anna_Teichm%C3%BCller "Anna Teichmüller") (_Die Prinzessin_). 
## Childhood and education
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Childhood and education")]
[![a painting of the farmyard in winter shows the barn, house and outbuildings around a central yard.](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fc/Bj%C3%B8rgan_by_Gerhard-Munthe.png/250px-Bj%C3%B8rgan_by_Gerhard-Munthe.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Bj%C3%B8rgan_by_Gerhard-Munthe.png)[Bjørgan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rgan_parsonage "Bjørgan parsonage") farmyard in Kvikne. Drawing by [Gerhard Munthe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gerhard_Munthe_\(painter\) "Gerhard Munthe \(painter\)")
Bjørnson was born at the farmstead of [Bjørgan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rgan_parsonage "Bjørgan parsonage") in [Kvikne](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kvikne_\(village\) "Kvikne \(village\)"), a secluded village in the [Østerdalen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%98sterdalen "Østerdalen") district, some 60 miles (97 km) south of [Trondheim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trondheim_\(city\) "Trondheim \(city\)"). In 1837, Bjørnson's father [Peder Bjørnson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peder_Bj%C3%B8rnson "Peder Bjørnson"), who was the [pastor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pastor "Pastor") of [Kvikne Municipality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kvikne_Municipality "Kvikne Municipality"), was transferred to the parish of [Nesset Municipality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nesset_Municipality "Nesset Municipality"), outside [Molde](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Molde_\(town\) "Molde \(town\)") in [Romsdal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romsdal "Romsdal"). It was in this scenic district that Bjørnson spent his childhood, living at the [Nesset Parsonage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nesset_Parsonage "Nesset Parsonage"). 
After a few years studying in the neighbouring city Molde, Bjørnson was sent at the age of 17 to [Heltberg Latin School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heltberg_Latin_School "Heltberg Latin School") [[no](https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heltbergs_Studentfabrik "no:Heltbergs Studentfabrik")] (_Heltbergs Studentfabrik_) in [Christiania](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oslo "Oslo") to prepare for university. This was the same school that trained [Ibsen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henrik_Ibsen "Henrik Ibsen"), [Lie](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jonas_Lie_\(writer\) "Jonas Lie \(writer\)"), and [Vinje](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aasmund_Olavsson_Vinje "Aasmund Olavsson Vinje"). 
Bjørnson had realized that he wanted to pursue his talent for poetry (he had written verses since age eleven). He matriculated at the [University of Oslo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Oslo "University of Oslo") in 1852, soon embarking upon a career as a journalist, focusing on criticism of drama.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-SNL-4)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-Nobel-5)
## Early production
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early production")]
In 1857, Bjørnson published _[Synnøve Solbakken](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Synn%C3%B8ve_Solbakken_\(novel\) "Synnøve Solbakken \(novel\)")_ , the first of his peasant novels. In 1858, this was followed by _[Arne](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arne_\(novel\) "Arne \(novel\)")_ , in 1860 by _[En glad Gut](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/En_glad_Gut "En glad Gut")_ (A Happy Boy), and in 1868 by _[Fiskerjenten](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Fiskerjenten&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fiskerjenten \(page does not exist\)") [[no](https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fiskerjenten "no:Fiskerjenten")]_ (The Fisher Girl). These are the most important specimens of his _bonde-fortellinger_ or peasant tales.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-PG-7) At least seven Danish composers wrote music based on _Arne_ : [Morten Eskesen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Morten_Eskesen "Morten Eskesen"), [C. J. Frydensberg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carl_Johan_Frydensberg "Carl Johan Frydensberg"), [Peter Heise](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peter_Arnold_Heise "Peter Arnold Heise"), Anton Nielsen, [Oluf Ring](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oluf_Ring "Oluf Ring"), [Henrik Rung](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henrik_Rung "Henrik Rung"), and [Sigrid Henriette Wienecke](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sigrid_Henriette_Wienecke "Sigrid Henriette Wienecke").[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-8)
Bjørnson was anxious "to create a new saga in the light of the peasant," as he put it, and he thought this should be done, not merely in prose fiction, but in national dramas or _folke-stykker_. The earliest of these was a one-act piece set in the 12th century, _[Mellem Slagene](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mellem_Slagene "Mellem Slagene")_ (Between the Battles), written in 1855 and produced in 1857. He was especially influenced at this time by the study of [Jens Immanuel Baggesen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jens_Immanuel_Baggesen "Jens Immanuel Baggesen") and [Adam Gottlob Oehlenschläger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adam_Gottlob_Oehlenschl%C3%A4ger "Adam Gottlob Oehlenschläger"), during a visit to [Copenhagen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Copenhagen "Copenhagen"). _Mellem Slagene_ was followed by _[Halte-Hulda](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halte-Hulda&action=edit&redlink=1 "Halte-Hulda \(page does not exist\)") [[no](https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halte-Hulda "no:Halte-Hulda"); [nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halte_Hulda "nl:Halte Hulda")]_ (Lame Hulda) in 1858, and _[Kong Sverre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sverre_of_Norway "Sverre of Norway")_ (King Sverre) in 1861.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6) His most important work to date was the poetic trilogy of _[Sigurd Slembe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sigurd_Slembe_\(trilogy\) "Sigurd Slembe \(trilogy\)")_ (Sigurd the Bad), which Bjørnson published in 1862.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-SNL-4)[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-PG-7)
## The mature author
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The mature author")]
At the close of 1857 Bjørnson had been appointed director of the theatre at [Bergen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bergen_\(city\) "Bergen \(city\)"), a post which he held for two years, when he returned to [Christiania](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oslo "Oslo"). From 1860 to 1863 he travelled widely throughout Europe. Early in 1865 he undertook the management of the [Christiania Theatre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christiania_Theatre "Christiania Theatre"),[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-9) and brought out his popular comedy of _[De Nygifte](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=De_Nygifte&action=edit&redlink=1 "De Nygifte \(page does not exist\)") [[no](https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/De_Nygifte "no:De Nygifte"); [sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/De_Nygifta "sv:De Nygifta"); [ar](https://ar.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%B9%D8%B1%D9%88%D8%B3%D8%A7%D9%86_\(%D9%85%D8%B3%D8%B1%D8%AD%D9%8A%D8%A9\) "ar:العروسان \(مسرحية\)"); [zh](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%96%B0%E5%A9%9A%E7%9A%84%E4%B8%80%E5%AF%B9 "zh:新婚的一对")]_ (The Newly Married) and his romantic tragedy of [Mary Stuart](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mary,_Queen_of_Scots "Mary, Queen of Scots") in Scotland. In 1870 he published _Poems and Songs_ and the epic cycle _Arnljot Gelline_ ; the latter volume contains the ode _Bergliot_ , one of Bjørnson's finest contributions to [lyrical poetry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lyrical_poetry "Lyrical poetry").[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
Between 1864 and 1874, Bjørnson displayed a slackening of the intellectual forces very remarkable in a man of his energy; he was mainly occupied with politics and with his business as a theatrical manager. This was the period of Bjørnson's most fiery propaganda as a radical agitator. In 1871 he began to supplement his journalistic work by delivering lectures throughout Scandinavia.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
From 1874 to 1876, Bjørnson was absent from Norway, and in the peace of voluntary exile he recovered his imaginative powers. His new departure as a dramatic author began with _[En fallit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/En_fallit "En fallit")_ (A Bankruptcy) and _Redaktøren_ (The Editor) in 1874, social dramas of an extremely modern and realistic cast.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
## Collaborations with Grieg
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Collaborations with Grieg")]
In the 1870's Bjørnson and the composer [Edvard Grieg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edvard_Grieg "Edvard Grieg"), who shared his interests in Norwegian self-government, became friends. Grieg set several of his poems to music, including _Landkjenning_ and _Sigurd Jorsalfar_.[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-10) Eventually they decided on an opera based on _King Olav Trygvason_ , but a dispute as to whether music or lyrics should be created first led to Grieg being diverted to working on incidental music for [Henrik Ibsen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henrik_Ibsen "Henrik Ibsen")'s play _[Peer Gynt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peer_Gynt "Peer Gynt")_ , which naturally offended Bjørnson. Eventually their friendship was resumed.[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-11)
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: The "national poet"")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/80/Bj%C3%B8rnson_bldsa_BB0786.jpg/250px-Bj%C3%B8rnson_bldsa_BB0786.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Bj%C3%B8rnson_bldsa_BB0786.jpg)Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson and Karoline Bjørnson at Aulestad
Bjørnson settled on his estate of [Aulestad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aulestad "Aulestad") in [Gausdal Municipality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gausdal_Municipality "Gausdal Municipality"). In 1877 he published another novel, _[Magnhild](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Magnhild_\(novel\)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Magnhild \(novel\) \(page does not exist\)")_ , in which his ideas on social questions were seen to be in a state of fermentation, and gave expression to his [republican](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Republicanism "Republicanism") sentiments in the polemical play _Kongen_ (The King). In a later edition of the play, he prefixed an essay on "[Intellectual Freedom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intellectual_freedom "Intellectual freedom")" in further explanation of his position. _[Kaptejn Mansana](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Kaptejn_Mansana&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kaptejn Mansana \(page does not exist\)")_ (Captain Mansana), an episode of the [war of Italian independence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification"), was written in to 1878.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
Extremely anxious to obtain full success on the stage, Bjørnson concentrated his powers on a drama of social life, _Leonarda_ (1879), which raised a violent controversy.[_[why?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")_] A satirical play, _Det nye System_ (The New System), was produced a few weeks later. Although these plays of Bjørnson's second period were greatly discussed, few were financially successful.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
Bjørnson produced a social drama, _En Handske_ (A Gauntlet), in 1883, but was unable to persuade any manager to stage it except in a modified form. In the autumn of the same year, Bjørnson published a mystical or symbolic drama, _[Over Ævne](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Over_%C3%86vne&action=edit&redlink=1 "Over Ævne \(page does not exist\)") [[no](https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/Over_%C3%86vne_I "no:Over Ævne I"); [fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Au-del%C3%A0_des_forces "fr:Au-delà des forces"); [nl](https://nl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Over_%C3%86vne "nl:Over Ævne"); [pl](https://pl.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ponad_si%C5%82y "pl:Ponad siły")]_ (Beyond Powers), dealing with the abnormal features of religious excitement with extraordinary force; this was not acted until 1899, when it achieved a great success.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
## Political interests
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Political interests")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/Bj%C3%B8rnson_blds_01655.jpg/250px-Bj%C3%B8rnson_blds_01655.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Bj%C3%B8rnson_blds_01655.jpg)Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson in 1908
From his youth and forwards, Bjørnson admired [Henrik Wergeland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henrik_Wergeland "Henrik Wergeland"), and became a vivid spokesman for the Norwegian left-wing movement. In this respect, he supported [Ivar Aasen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ivar_Aasen "Ivar Aasen"), and joined forces in the political struggles in the 1860s and 1870s. When the great monument over Henrik Wergeland was to be erected in 1881, it came to political struggle between left and right, and the left wing got the upper hand. Bjørnson presented the speech on behalf of Wergeland, and also honouring the constitution and the farmers.[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-Groendahl-3)
Bjørnson's political opinions[_[clarification needed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")_] had brought upon him a charge of high treason,[_[why?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")_] and he took refuge for a time in Germany, returning to Norway in 1882.[_[how?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")_] Convinced that the theatre was practically closed to him,[_[why?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")_] he turned back to the novel, and published in 1884 _Det flager i Byen og paa Havnen_ (Flags are Flying in Town and Port), embodying his theories on heredity and education. In 1889 he printed another long and still more remarkable novel, _[Paa Guds Veje](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Paa_Guds_Veje&action=edit&redlink=1 "Paa Guds Veje \(page does not exist\)") [[no](https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paa_Guds_Veje "no:Paa Guds Veje")]_ (On God's Path), which is chiefly concerned with the same problems. The same year saw the publication of a comedy, _[Geografi og Kærlighed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geografi_og_K%C3%A6rlighed "Geografi og Kærlighed")_ (Geography and Love), which met with success.[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-Groendahl-3)[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
A number of short stories, of a more or less [didactic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Didactic "Didactic") character, dealing with startling points of emotional experience, were collected and published 1894. Later plays were a political tragedy called _[Paul Lange og Tora Parsberg](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Paul_Lange_og_Tora_Parsberg&action=edit&redlink=1 "Paul Lange og Tora Parsberg \(page does not exist\)") [[no](https://no.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Lange_og_Tora_Parsberg "no:Paul Lange og Tora Parsberg")]_ (1898), a second part of _Over Ævne_ (Beyond Powers II) (1895), _Laboremus_ (1901), _På Storhove_ (At Storhove) (1902), and _Daglannet_ (Dag's Farm) (1904). In 1899, at the opening of the National Theatre, Bjørnson received an ovation, and his saga-drama of King [Sigurd the Crusader](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sigurd_I_of_Norway "Sigurd I of Norway") was performed at the opening of [Nationaltheatret](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nationaltheatret "Nationaltheatret") in Oslo.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6)
Bjørnson was one of the contributors of the anti-Union magazine _[Ringeren](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ringeren "Ringeren")_ , edited by [Sigurd Ibsen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sigurd_Ibsen "Sigurd Ibsen") in 1898.[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-12)
A subject which interested him greatly was the question of the _[bondemaal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nynorsk "Nynorsk")_ , the adopting of a national language for Norway distinct from the _dansk-norsk_ ([Dano-Norwegian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dano-Norwegian "Dano-Norwegian")), in which most Norwegian literature had hitherto been written.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6) At an early stage, before 1860, Bjørnson had himself experimented with at least one short story written in [landsmål](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nynorsk "Nynorsk"). The interest, however, did not last, and he soon abandoned this enterprise altogether. Afterwards, he regretted that he never felt he gained the mastery of this language. Bjørnson's strong and sometimes rather narrow patriotism did not blind him to what he considered the fatal folly of such a proposal, and his lectures and pamphlets against the _målstræv_ 'language strife' in its extreme form were very effective. 
His attitude towards this must have changed sometime after 1881, as he still spoke on behalf of the farmers at this point. Although he seems to have been supportive of [Ivar Aasen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ivar_Aasen "Ivar Aasen") and friendly towards farmers (in the peasant novels), he later denounced this, and stated in 1899 that there was limits to a farmer's cultivation. "I can draw a line on the wall. The farmer can cultivate himself to this level, and no more", he wrote in 1899. Rumour has it that he had been insulted by a farmer at some point, and uttered the statement in sheer anger. In 1881, he spoke of the farmer's clothing borne by Henrik Wergeland, and his opinion then states that this garment, worn by Wergeland, was "of the most influential things" in the initiation of the national day. Bjørnson's attitude towards the farmers remain ambiguous. His father himself was a farmer's son. During the last twenty years of his life he wrote hundreds of articles in major European papers. He attacked the French justice in the [Dreyfus Affair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dreyfus_affair "Dreyfus affair"), and he fought for the rights of children in Slovakia to learn their own mother tongue. "To detach children from their mother tongue is identical to tearing them away from their mothers' breasts," he wrote. 
Bjørnson wrote in multiple newspapers about the [Černová massacre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C4%8Cernov%C3%A1_massacre "Černová massacre") under the title _The greatest industry of Hungary_ – which was supposedly "[to produce Magyars](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magyarization "Magyarization")". 
He took part in the [sexual morality debate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nordic_sexual_morality_debate "Nordic sexual morality debate") (_sedelighetsdebatten_) of the time, arguing that free love did not allow for the development of positive traits such as self-restraint and a focus on virtue. Bjørnson held some 60 lectures in the Nordic countries on the issue; his strongly held views led to a rift with [Georg Brandes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Georg_Brandes "Georg Brandes").[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-13)
## Last years
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Last years")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/En_politisk_telegramveksling.jpg/250px-En_politisk_telegramveksling.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:En_politisk_telegramveksling.jpg)Illustration from _[Vikingen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vikingen "Vikingen")_ of a telegram exchange between Michelsen and Bjørnson.
Bjørnson was, from the beginning of the [Dreyfus Affair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dreyfus_Affair "Dreyfus Affair"), a staunch supporter of [Alfred Dreyfus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alfred_Dreyfus "Alfred Dreyfus"), and, according to a contemporary, wrote "article after article in the papers and proclaimed in every manner his belief in his innocence". 
Bjørnson was one of the original members of the [Norwegian Nobel Committee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norwegian_Nobel_Committee "Norwegian Nobel Committee"), that awards the [Nobel Peace Prize](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobel_Peace_Prize "Nobel Peace Prize"), where he sat from 1901 to 1906.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-14) In 1903 he was awarded the [Nobel Prize in Literature](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nobel_Prize_in_Literature "Nobel Prize in Literature"). 
In 1901, Bjørnson proclaimed, "I'm a [Pan-Germanist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pan-Germanism "Pan-Germanism"), I'm a [Teuton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teutonic_peoples "Teutonic peoples"), and the greatest dream of my life is for the [South Germanic peoples](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Germanic_languages "West Germanic languages") and the [North Germanic peoples](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_Germanic_peoples "North Germanic peoples") and their brothers in [diaspora](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diaspora "Diaspora") to unite in a fellow [confederation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greater_Germanic_Reich "Greater Germanic Reich")."[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-dagbladet.no-15)
Bjørnson had done as much as any other man to rouse Norwegian nationalistic feeling, but in 1903, on the verge of the rupture between Norway and Sweden, he preached conciliation and moderation to the Norwegians.[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGosse1911-6) However, in 1905 he largely remained silent. 
When Norway was attempting to dissolve the forced union with Sweden, Bjørnson sent a telegram to the Norwegian Prime minister stating, "Now is the time to unite." The minister replied, "Now is the time to shut up."[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-Groendahl-3) This was in fact a satirical illustration published in _[Vikingen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vikingen "Vikingen")_ , but the story got so popular and widespread that Bjørnson had to deny it, claiming that "Michelsen has never asked me to shut up; it would not help if he did".[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-16)
He died on 26 April 1910 in Paris, where for some years he had spent his winters, and was buried at home with every mark of honour. The Norwegian [coastal defence ship](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coastal_defence_ship "Coastal defence ship") [HNoMS  _Norge_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HNoMS_Norge "HNoMS Norge") was sent to convey his remains back to his own land. 
## Family and relationships
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Family and relationships")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson_and_his_Family.jpg/250px-Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson_and_his_Family.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson_and_his_Family.jpg)Bjørnstjerne Bjørnson and his family, 1882.
Bjørnson was the son of the Reverend Mr. Peder Bjørnson and Inger Elise Nordraach. He married [Karoline Reimers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karoline_Reimers "Karoline Reimers") (1835–1934) in 1858.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-SNL-4) They had six children, five of whom lived to adulthood: [Bjørn Bjørnson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rn_Bj%C3%B8rnson "Bjørn Bjørnson") (1859–1942), Einar Bjørnson (1864–1942), [Erling Bjørnson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Erling_Bj%C3%B8rnson "Erling Bjørnson") (1868–1959), [Bergliot Bjørnson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bergliot_Ibsen "Bergliot Ibsen") (1869–1953), Dagny Bjørnson (1871–1872), Dagny Bjørnson (1876–1974).[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-:0-17)
In 1860, Bjørnson met and began an affair with [Magda von Dolcke](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Magda_von_Dolcke "Magda von Dolcke") while he was in [Denmark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denmark "Denmark"). Just two years after he had been married, his wife Karoline was in Norway at the time, recovering from a fever she had contracted after the birth of their first son in November 1859. Even after he returned to Norway, Bjørnson continued to exchange intimate letters with Dolcke and described their relationship as a "Pagt med en Sjel" (English: _Pact with a Soul_). His affair with Dolcke ended after he reconciled with Karoline in late 1861.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-18)
In his early fifties, Bjørnson had an affair with 17-year-old Guri Andersdotter (died 1949), which resulted in the birth of their son, [Anders Underdal](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Anders_Underdal&action=edit&redlink=1 "Anders Underdal \(page does not exist\)") (1880–1973). The affair was kept a secret, though early on Anders Underdal, a poet, would talk about his origins with his children. Later in life he stopped discussing the matter, no reason was given. Anders was the father of Norwegian-Swedish author [Margit Sandemo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Margit_Sandemo "Margit Sandemo"). Audun Thorsen has written a book about Bjørnson's affair, _Bjørnsons kvinne og Margit Sandemos "familiehemmelighet"_ (lit.'Bjørnson's woman and Margit Sandemo's 'family secret'') (Genesis forlag, Oslo 1999). 
[Karoline Bjørnson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karoline_Bj%C3%B8rnson "Karoline Bjørnson") remained at [Aulestad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aulestad "Aulestad") until her death in 1934.[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bj%C3%B8rnstjerne_Bj%C3%B8rnson#cite_note-:0-17)

Support Local Services With The Metallica Family! The goal of supporting critical local services is to create healthy communities. AWMH partners with Direct Relief and Team Rubicon to provide essential disaster relief and recovery resources. While on tour, Metallica also donates to local shelters that provide safe, reliable housing and services. For more info on All Within My Hands, visit [https://www.allwithinmyhands.org](https://www.youtube.com/redirect?event=video_description&redir_token=QUFFLUhqbnFjbnlBTEJEWktPU2ZRX2F4bkNrZlU4QVJRQXxBQ3Jtc0trbTQ1bURhRkVNUG00VVVYSVVmZlgzanZ3Z0x1Z005dy1VNWdUR2xMVHhyeXpxNEREUmJDQmhCYkhvellLdUhZRWIwOWszTnlSczVnbWJ6WGZOLUVIRmE5blR2TTZNd2ZtSTFzQlN5dHBoQVd1VVNOUQ&q=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.allwithinmyhands.org%2F). **Metallica** is an American [heavy metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heavy_metal_music "Heavy metal music") band. It was formed in [Los Angeles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") in 1981 by vocalist/guitarist [James Hetfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Hetfield "James Hetfield") and drummer [Lars Ulrich](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lars_Ulrich "Lars Ulrich"), and has been based in [San Francisco](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco") for most of its career.[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-SF-1)[[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-MetallicaOfficial-2) The band's fast tempos, instrumentals and aggressive musicianship made them one of the founding "big four" bands of [thrash metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thrash_metal "Thrash metal"), alongside [Megadeth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Megadeth "Megadeth"), [Anthrax](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthrax_\(American_band\) "Anthrax \(American band\)"), and [Slayer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slayer "Slayer"). Metallica's current lineup comprises founding members and primary songwriters Hetfield and Ulrich, longtime lead guitarist [Kirk Hammett](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kirk_Hammett "Kirk Hammett"), and bassist [Robert Trujillo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Trujillo "Robert Trujillo"). Former members of the band are bassists [Ron McGovney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ron_McGovney "Ron McGovney"), [Cliff Burton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cliff_Burton "Cliff Burton"), and [Jason Newsted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jason_Newsted "Jason Newsted"), and guitarist [Dave Mustaine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dave_Mustaine "Dave Mustaine"), who formed Megadeth after being fired from Metallica in 1983. 
Metallica first found commercial success with the release of its third album, _[Master of Puppets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Master_of_Puppets "Master of Puppets")_ (1986), which is often cited as one of the best metal albums of all time. The band's next album, _[...And Justice for All](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/...And_Justice_for_All_\(album\) "...And Justice for All \(album\)")_ (1988), gave Metallica its first [Grammy Award](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") nomination. Its fifth album, _[Metallica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica_\(album\) "Metallica \(album\)")_ (1991), was a turning point for the band that saw it transition from its thrash metal roots; the album appealed to a more mainstream audience, achieving substantial commercial success and selling more than 16 million copies in the United States to date, making it the best-selling album of the [SoundScan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SoundScan "SoundScan") era. After experimenting with different genres and directions in subsequent releases, Metallica returned to its thrash metal roots with its ninth album, _[Death Magnetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Death_Magnetic "Death Magnetic")_ (2008), which drew similar praise to that of the band's earlier albums. The band's eleventh and most recent album, _[72 Seasons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/72_Seasons "72 Seasons")_ , was released in 2023. 
In 2000, [Metallica led the case](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica_v._Napster,_Inc. "Metallica v. Napster, Inc.") against the peer-to-peer file sharing service [Napster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Napster "Napster"), in which the band and several other artists filed lawsuits against the service for sharing their copyright-protected material without consent, eventually reaching a settlement. Metallica was the subject of the acclaimed 2004 documentary film _[Metallica: Some Kind of Monster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica:_Some_Kind_of_Monster "Metallica: Some Kind of Monster")_ , which documented the troubled production of the band's eighth album, _[St. Anger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Anger "St. Anger")_ (2003), and the internal struggles within the band at the time. In 2009, Metallica was inducted into the [Rock and Roll Hall of Fame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock_and_Roll_Hall_of_Fame "Rock and Roll Hall of Fame"). The band co-wrote the screenplay for and starred alongside [Dane DeHaan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dane_DeHaan "Dane DeHaan") in the 2013 concert film _[Metallica: Through the Never](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica:_Through_the_Never "Metallica: Through the Never")_ , in which the band performed live against a fictional thriller storyline. 
Metallica is one of the [most commercially successful bands of all time](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists"), having sold more than 163 million albums worldwide as of 2023[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Metallica&action=edit),[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-sold-3) and is the third-best-selling music artist since Nielsen SoundScan began tracking sales in 1991,[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Album_Sales_1-4) having sold 58 million albums in the United States as of 2017[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Metallica&action=edit).[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-5) The band has released eleven studio albums, four live albums (including two performances with the [San Francisco Symphony](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco_Symphony "San Francisco Symphony")), twelve video albums, one cover album, two extended plays, 37 singles and 39 music videos. Metallica has won ten Grammy Awards from 26 nominations and had six consecutive studio albums, from _Metallica_ through _[Hardwired... to Self-Destruct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hardwired..._to_Self-Destruct "Hardwired... to Self-Destruct")_ (2016), debut at number one on the [_Billboard_ 200](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"). Metallica has been listed as one of the greatest artists of all time by magazines such as _[Rolling Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")_ , which ranked the band in 61st place on its [list of 100 greatest artists of all time](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolling_Stone%27s_100_Greatest_Artists_of_All_Time "Rolling Stone's 100 Greatest Artists of All Time").[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-6)
## History
### _Kill 'Em All_
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ed/Metallica_Of_Wolf_and_Man_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Metallica_Of_Wolf_and_Man_%28cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_Of_Wolf_and_Man_\(cropped\).jpg)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Lars_Ulrich_%28Metallica%29.jpg/250px-Lars_Ulrich_%28Metallica%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Lars_Ulrich_\(Metallica\).jpg)
Metallica founding members James Hetfield (top) and Lars Ulrich (bottom)
Metallica was formed in [Los Angeles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") in late 1981 when Danish drummer [Lars Ulrich](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lars_Ulrich "Lars Ulrich") placed an advertisement in a Los Angeles newspaper, _[The Recycler](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Recycler "The Recycler")_ , in which the advertisement read, "Drummer looking for other metal musicians to jam with [Tygers of Pan Tang](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tygers_of_Pan_Tang "Tygers of Pan Tang"), [Diamond Head](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diamond_Head_\(English_band\) "Diamond Head \(English band\)") and [Iron Maiden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iron_Maiden "Iron Maiden")."[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_1-7) Guitarists [James Hetfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Hetfield "James Hetfield") and Hugh Tanner of [Leather Charm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leather_Charm "Leather Charm") answered the advertisement. Although he had not formed a band, Ulrich asked [Metal Blade Records](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metal_Blade_Records "Metal Blade Records") founder [Brian Slagel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Slagel "Brian Slagel") if he could record a song for the label's upcoming compilation album, _[Metal Massacre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metal_Massacre "Metal Massacre")_. Slagel accepted.[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_1-7) The band was officially formed on October 28, 1981, five months after Ulrich and Hetfield first met.[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-8)[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-9)
The band name came from Ulrich's friend Ron Quintana, who was thinking of names for a [fanzine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fanzine "Fanzine") and was considering _MetalMania_ or _Metallica_. After hearing the two names, Ulrich wanted the latter for his band, so he suggested Quintana use _MetalMania_ instead.[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-10) [Dave Mustaine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dave_Mustaine "Dave Mustaine") replied to an advertisement for a lead guitarist; Ulrich and Hetfield recruited him after seeing his expensive guitar equipment.[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_1-7)
Hetfield took vocal duties at the band's first gig, which took place in March 1982 at Radio City in [Anaheim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anaheim "Anaheim")[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-11) with newly recruited bassist [Ron McGovney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ron_McGovney "Ron McGovney").[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-12) They were chosen to open for British heavy metal band [Saxon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_\(band\) "Saxon \(band\)") at the second gig of their 1982 U.S. tour.[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-auto1-13)
Metallica recorded its first demo, _[Power Metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_Metal_\(Metallica_album\) "Power Metal \(Metallica album\)")_ , whose name was inspired by McGovney's early business cards, in early 1982.[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-auto1-13) The band played as a five-piece at their fourth gig, which took place in April 1982 in [Costa Mesa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Costa_Mesa "Costa Mesa") with lead guitarist Brad Parker, though he did not last.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-14) According to Mustaine, the band was "fed up" with auditioning guitar players and Hetfield became the de facto guitarist, in addition to the vocalist.[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-15) Hetfield played guitar live for the first time at the band's fifth gig, in May 1982.[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-16) _Metal Massacre I_ was released on June 14, 1982; early pressings listed the band incorrectly as "Mettallica", which angered the band.[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-17) Hetfield sang and played both bass and rhythm guitar, while Lars Ulrich played drums[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_1-7) and Lloyd Grant played a guitar solo.[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-18)
The term "[thrash metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thrash_metal "Thrash metal")" was coined in February 1984 by _[Kerrang!](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kerrang! "Kerrang!")_ journalist [Malcolm Dome](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malcolm_Dome "Malcolm Dome") in reference to [Anthrax](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthrax_\(American_band\) "Anthrax \(American band\)")'s song "[Metal Thrashing Mad](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metal_Thrashing_Mad "Metal Thrashing Mad")".[[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-19) Prior to this, Hetfield referred to Metallica's sound as "[power metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_metal "Power metal")". In late 1982, Ulrich and Hetfield attended a show at the [West Hollywood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Hollywood "West Hollywood") nightclub [Whisky a Go Go](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Whisky_a_Go_Go "Whisky a Go Go"), which featured bassist [Cliff Burton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cliff_Burton "Cliff Burton") in the band [Trauma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trauma_\(American_band\) "Trauma \(American band\)"). The two were "blown away" by Burton's use of a [wah-wah pedal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wah-wah_pedal "Wah-wah pedal") and asked him to join Metallica. Hetfield and Mustaine wanted McGovney to leave because they thought he "didn't contribute anything, he just followed."[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_2-20) Although Burton initially declined the offer, by the end of the year, he had accepted on the condition that the band move to [El Cerrito](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Cerrito,_California "El Cerrito, California") in the [San Francisco Bay Area](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco_Bay_Area "San Francisco Bay Area").[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_2-20) Metallica's first live performance with Burton was at the nightclub The Stone in March 1983, and the first recording to feature Burton was the _Megaforce_ demo (1983).[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_2-20)
Metallica was ready to record their debut album, but when Metal Blade was unable to cover the cost, they began looking for other options. Concert promoter [Jonathan "Jonny Z" Zazula](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jon_Zazula "Jon Zazula"), who had heard the demo _No Life 'til Leather_ (1982), offered to broker a record deal between Metallica and [New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City")–based record labels. After those record labels showed no interest, Zazula borrowed enough money to cover the recording budget and signed Metallica to his own label, [Megaforce Records](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Megaforce_Records "Megaforce Records").[[21]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Pillsbury_2006_2-21)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Priest_feast_22_-_megadeth_05.jpg/250px-Priest_feast_22_-_megadeth_05.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Priest_feast_22_-_megadeth_05.jpg)Dave Mustaine (pictured in 2009) went on to found rival band Megadeth after being fired from the band in 1983.
In May 1983, Metallica traveled to [Rochester, New York](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"), to record its debut album, _Metal Up Your Ass_ , which was produced by Paul Curcio.[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-22) The other members decided to eject Mustaine from the band because of his drug and alcohol abuse and violent behavior just before the recording sessions on April 11, 1983.[[23]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-23) [Exodus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Exodus_\(band\) "Exodus \(band\)") guitarist [Kirk Hammett](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kirk_Hammett "Kirk Hammett") replaced Mustaine the same afternoon.[[21]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Pillsbury_2006_2-21) Metallica's first live performance with Hammett was on April 16, 1983, at a nightclub in [Dover, New Jersey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dover,_New_Jersey "Dover, New Jersey"), called The Showplace;[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_2-20) the support act was Anthrax, which included [Dan Lilker](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dan_Lilker "Dan Lilker") and [Neil Turbin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neil_Turbin "Neil Turbin").[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-24) This was the first time the two bands performed live together.[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_2-20)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/07/Kirk2010.jpg/250px-Kirk2010.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Kirk2010.jpg)Kirk Hammett (pictured in 2010) replaced Mustaine in 1983, and has been with the band ever since.
Mustaine, who went on to form [Megadeth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Megadeth "Megadeth"), has expressed his dislike for Hammett in interviews, saying Hammett "stole" his job.[[25]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-25)
Because of conflicts with its record label and the distributors' refusal to release an album titled _Metal Up Your Ass_ , the album was renamed _[Kill 'Em All](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kill_%27Em_All "Kill 'Em All")_. It was released on Megaforce Records in the U.S. and on [Music for Nations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Music_for_Nations "Music for Nations") in Europe, and peaked at number 155 on the _Billboard_ 200 in 1986.[[26]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Whitburn-26)[[a]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-27) Although the album was not initially a financial success, it earned Metallica a growing fan base in the underground metal scene.[[27]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-28) To support the release, Metallica embarked on the _Kill 'Em All for One_ tour with [Raven](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raven_\(British_band\) "Raven \(British band\)").[[28]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-29) In February 1984, Metallica supported [Venom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venom_\(band\) "Venom \(band\)") on the Seven Dates of Hell tour, during which the bands performed in front of 7,000 people at the Aardschok Festival in [Zwolle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zwolle "Zwolle"), Netherlands.[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_3-30)
###  _Ride the Lightning_ , _Master of Puppets_ , and Burton's death
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cb/Metallica_1983_press_photo_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Metallica_1983_press_photo_%28cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_1983_press_photo_\(cropped\).jpg)[Cliff Burton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cliff_Burton "Cliff Burton") (pictured in 1983) replaced Ron McGovney as the bassist in 1982 and played with the band until his death in 1986.
Metallica recorded its second studio album, _[Ride the Lightning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ride_the_Lightning "Ride the Lightning")_ , at [Sweet Silence Studios](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sweet_Silence_Studios "Sweet Silence Studios") in [Copenhagen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Copenhagen "Copenhagen"), Denmark, from February to March 1984. It was released on July 27, 1984 and reached number 100 on the _Billboard_ 200.[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Charts-31) A French printing press mistakenly printed green covers for the album, which are now considered collector's items. Mustaine received writing co-credit for "Ride the Lightning" and "The Call of Ktulu".[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_3-30)
[Elektra Records](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elektra_Records "Elektra Records") [A&R](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A%26R "A&R") director Michael Alago and co-founder of [Q Prime](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Q_Prime "Q Prime") management, [Cliff Burnstein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cliff_Burnstein "Cliff Burnstein"), attended a Metallica concert in September 1984; they were impressed with the performance, signed Metallica to Elektra and made the band a client of Q Prime.[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_4-32) Metallica's growing success was such that the band's British label, Music for Nations, released "[Creeping Death](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creeping_Death "Creeping Death")" as a limited-edition single, which sold 40,000 copies as an import in the U.S. Two of the three songs on the record – cover versions of Diamond Head's "[Am I Evil?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Am_I_Evil%3F "Am I Evil?")" and [Blitzkrieg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blitzkrieg_\(metal_band\) "Blitzkrieg \(metal band\)")'s "Blitzkrieg" – appeared on the 1988 Elektra reissue of _Kill 'Em All_.[[32]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-33) Metallica embarked on its first major European tour with [Tank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tank_\(band\) "Tank \(band\)") to an average crowd of 1,300. Returning to the U.S., it embarked on a co-headlining tour with [W.A.S.P.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/W.A.S.P._\(band\) "W.A.S.P. \(band\)") and support from [Armored Saint](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Armored_Saint "Armored Saint"). Metallica played its largest show at the [Monsters of Rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monsters_of_Rock "Monsters of Rock") festival at [Donington Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donington_Park_motorsport_circuit#Music_festivals "Donington Park motorsport circuit"), England, on August 17, 1985, with [Bon Jovi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bon_Jovi "Bon Jovi") and [Ratt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ratt "Ratt"), playing to 70,000 people. At a show in [Oakland, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oakland,_California "Oakland, California"), at the [Day on the Green](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Day_on_the_Green "Day on the Green") festival, the band played to a crowd of 60,000.[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_4-32)
Metallica's third studio album, _[Master of Puppets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Master_of_Puppets "Master of Puppets")_ , was recorded at [Sweet Silence Studios](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sweet_Silence_Studios "Sweet Silence Studios") from September to December 1985 and released on March 3, 1986. The album reached number 29 on the [_Billboard_ 200](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") and spent 72 weeks on the chart.[[33]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-34) It was the band's first album to be certified [Gold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Music_recording_sales_certification#RIAA_certification "Music recording sales certification") on November 4, 1986, and was certified eight times Platinum in 2025.[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-35) Steve Huey of [AllMusic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AllMusic "AllMusic") considered the album "the band's greatest achievement".[[35]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-36) Following the release of the album, Metallica supported [Ozzy Osbourne](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ozzy_Osbourne "Ozzy Osbourne") on a U.S. tour.[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_4-32) Hetfield broke his wrist while skateboarding; he continued with the tour, performing vocals, with guitar technician [John Marshall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Marshall_\(guitarist\) "John Marshall \(guitarist\)") playing rhythm guitar.[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_5-37)
On September 27, 1986, during the European leg of Metallica's [Damage, Inc. Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Damage,_Inc._Tour "Damage, Inc. Tour"), members drew cards to determine which bunks on the tour bus they would sleep in. Burton won and chose to sleep in Hammett's bunk. At around sunrise near [Dörarp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/D%C3%B6rarp "Dörarp"), Sweden, the bus driver lost control and skidded, which caused the bus to overturn several times. Ulrich, Hammett and Hetfield sustained no serious injuries; however, Burton was pinned under the bus and died. Hetfield said: 
> I saw the bus lying right on him. I saw his legs sticking out. I freaked. The bus driver, I recall, was trying to yank the blanket out from under him to use for other people. I just went, "Don't fucking do that!" I already wanted to kill the [bus driver]. I don't know if he was drunk or if he hit some ice. All I knew was, he was driving and Cliff wasn't alive anymore.[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_5-37)
###  _...And Justice for All_ and _Metallica_
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b8/13-06-09_RaR_Newsted_13.jpg/250px-13-06-09_RaR_Newsted_13.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:13-06-09_RaR_Newsted_13.jpg)Jason Newsted (pictured in 2013) joined Metallica soon after Cliff Burton's death in 1986.
Burton's death left Metallica's future in doubt. The three remaining members decided Burton would want them to carry on, and with the Burton family's blessings, the band sought a replacement.[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_6-38) Roughly 40 people – including Hammett's childhood friend, [Les Claypool](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Les_Claypool "Les Claypool") of [Primus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Primus_\(band\) "Primus \(band\)"); [Troy Gregory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Troy_Gregory "Troy Gregory") of [Prong](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prong_\(band\) "Prong \(band\)"); and [Jason Newsted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jason_Newsted "Jason Newsted"), formerly of [Flotsam and Jetsam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flotsam_and_Jetsam_\(band\) "Flotsam and Jetsam \(band\)") – auditioned for the band to fill Burton's spot. Newsted learned Metallica's entire setlist; after the audition, Metallica invited him to Tommy's Joynt in San Francisco. Hetfield, Ulrich and Hammett decided on Newsted as Burton's replacement; his first live performance with Metallica was at the Country Club in [Reseda, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reseda,_California "Reseda, California"). The members initiated Newsted by tricking him into eating a ball of [wasabi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wasabi "Wasabi").[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_6-38) The band finished its tour in February 1987. 
After Newsted joined Metallica, the band left their El Cerrito practice space – a suburban house formerly rented by sound engineer [Mark Whitaker](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_Whitaker_\(music_producer\) "Mark Whitaker \(music producer\)") and dubbed "the Metalli-mansion" – and relocated to the adjacent cities of [Berkeley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Berkeley,_California "Berkeley, California") and [Albany](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albany,_California "Albany, California") before eventually settling in the Marin County city of [San Rafael](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Rafael,_California "San Rafael, California"), north of San Francisco.[[38]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-39)[[39]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-40) In March 1987, Hetfield again broke his wrist while skateboarding, forcing the band to cancel an appearance on _[Saturday Night Live](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")_. On August 21, 1987, an all-covers [extended play](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") (EP), titled _[The $5.98 E.P. - Garage Days Re-Revisited](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_$5.98_E.P._-_Garage_Days_Re-Revisited "The $5.98 E.P. - Garage Days Re-Revisited")_ , was released. The EP was recorded in an effort to use the band's newly constructed recording studio, test Newsted's talents, and to relieve grief and stress following the death of Burton. A video titled _[Cliff 'Em All](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cliff_%27Em_All "Cliff 'Em All")_ commemorating Burton's three years in Metallica was released in 1987; the video included bass solos, home videos and pictures.[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-41)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d6/Metallica_Damaged_Justice_Tour.jpg/250px-Metallica_Damaged_Justice_Tour.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_Damaged_Justice_Tour.jpg)Metallica performing during its Damaged Justice Tour in 1988
Metallica's first studio album since Burton's death, _[...And Justice for All](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/...And_Justice_for_All_\(album\) "...And Justice for All \(album\)")_ , was recorded from January to May 1988 and released in September. The album was a commercial success, reaching number 6 on the _Billboard_ 200, and was the band's first album to enter the top 10.[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Charts-31) The album was certified Platinum nine weeks after its release.[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-42) There were complaints about the production, however; Steve Huey of AllMusic said Ulrich's drums were clicking more than thudding, and the guitars "buzz thinly".[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-43) The lack of audible bass in the album also drew widespread criticism.[[43]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-44) To promote the album, Metallica embarked on a tour called [Damaged Justice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Damaged_Justice "Damaged Justice").[[44]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-45)
In 1989, Metallica received its first [Grammy Award](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") nomination for _...And Justice for All_ in the new [Best Hard Rock/Metal Performance Vocal or Instrument](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Hard_Rock/Metal_Performance_Vocal_or_Instrumental "Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock/Metal Performance Vocal or Instrumental") category. Metallica was the favorite to win, but the award was given to [Jethro Tull](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jethro_Tull_\(band\) "Jethro Tull \(band\)") for the album _[Crest of a Knave](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crest_of_a_Knave "Crest of a Knave")_.[[45]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Rockin'_on_an_Island-46) The award was controversial with fans and the press; Metallica was standing off-stage waiting to receive the award after performing the song "[One](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One_\(Metallica_song\) "One \(Metallica song\)")". Jethro Tull had been advised by its manager not to attend the ceremony because he was expecting Metallica to win.[[45]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Rockin'_on_an_Island-46) The award was named in _[Entertainment Weekly](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly "Entertainment Weekly")_ 's "Grammy's 10 Biggest Upsets" list.[[46]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-47)
Following the release of _...And Justice for All_ , Metallica released its debut music video for the song "One", which the band performed in an abandoned warehouse. The footage was remixed with the film _[Johnny Got His Gun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Johnny_Got_His_Gun_\(film\) "Johnny Got His Gun \(film\)")_. Rather than organize an ongoing licensing deal, Metallica purchased the rights to the film. The remixed video was submitted to MTV with an alternative, performance-only version that was held back in case MTV banned the remixed version. MTV accepted the remixed version; the video was viewers' first exposure to Metallica. In 1999, it was voted number 38 in MTV's "Top 100 Videos of All Time" countdown[[47]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-48) and was featured on the network's 25th-anniversary edition of _ADD Video_ , which showcased the most popular videos on MTV in the last 25 years.[[48]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-49)
In October 1990, Metallica entered [One on One Recording](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One_on_One_Recording "One on One Recording")'s studio in North Hollywood to record its next album. [Bob Rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bob_Rock "Bob Rock"), who had worked with [Aerosmith](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aerosmith "Aerosmith"), [the Cult](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Cult "The Cult"), [Bon Jovi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bon_Jovi "Bon Jovi") and [Mötley Crüe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M%C3%B6tley_Cr%C3%BCe "Mötley Crüe"), was hired as the producer. _[Metallica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica_\(album\) "Metallica \(album\)")_ – also known as _The Black Album_ – was remixed three times, cost US$1 million,[[49]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_8-50) and ended Hammett and Ulrich's marriages.[[50]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-51) Although the release was delayed until 1991, _Metallica_ debuted at number one in 10 countries, selling 650,000 units in the U.S. during its first week.[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-52) The album marked a change in Metallica's musical style and brought the band mainstream attention; it has been certified 16 times Platinum in the U.S., which makes it the 25th-best-selling album in the country.[[52]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Top_100_Albums-53) The making of _Metallica_ and the following tour was documented in _[A Year and a Half in the Life of Metallica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_Year_and_a_Half_in_the_Life_of_Metallica "A Year and a Half in the Life of Metallica")_. The tour in support of the album, called the [Wherever We May Roam Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wherever_We_May_Roam_Tour "Wherever We May Roam Tour"), lasted 14 months and included dates in the U.S., Japan and the U.K.[[49]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_8-50) In September 1991, 1.6 million rock music fans converged in [Moscow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moscow "Moscow") to enjoy the first open-air rock concert to be held in the [Soviet Union](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union"); it was part of the [Monsters of Rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monsters_of_Rock "Monsters of Rock") series.[[53]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-54) In April 1992, Metallica appeared at [The Freddie Mercury Tribute Concert](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Freddie_Mercury_Tribute_Concert "The Freddie Mercury Tribute Concert") and performed a three-song set.[[54]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Purvis-55) Hetfield later performed "[Stone Cold Crazy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stone_Cold_Crazy "Stone Cold Crazy")" with the remaining members of [Queen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queen_\(band\) "Queen \(band\)") and [Tony Iommi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tony_Iommi "Tony Iommi").[[54]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Purvis-55)
On August 8, 1992, during the co-headlining [Guns N' Roses/Metallica Stadium Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guns_N%27_Roses/Metallica_Stadium_Tour "Guns N' Roses/Metallica Stadium Tour") at the [Olympic Stadium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Olympic_Stadium_\(Montreal\) "Olympic Stadium \(Montreal\)") in [Montreal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"), Hetfield suffered second and [third-degree burns](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third-degree_burns "Third-degree burns") to his arms, face, hands and legs. There had been some confusion with the new pyrotechnics setup, which resulted in Hetfield walking into a 12-foot (3.7 m) flame during "Fade to Black".[[55]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-56) Newsted said Hetfield's skin was "bubbling like on _[The Toxic Avenger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Toxic_Avenger_\(1984_film\) "The Toxic Avenger \(1984 film\)")_ ".[[56]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_9-57) Metallica returned to the stage seventeen days later with guitar technician and [Metal Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metal_Church "Metal Church") member [John Marshall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Marshall_\(guitarist\) "John Marshall \(guitarist\)") replacing Hetfield on guitar for the remainder of the tour, although Hetfield was able to sing. Later in 1993, Metallica went on the Nowhere Else to Roam Tour, playing five shows in [Mexico City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"). _[Live Shit: Binge& Purge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Live_Shit:_Binge_%26_Purge "Live Shit: Binge & Purge")_, the band's first box set, was released in November 1993. The collection contains three live CDs, three home videos, and a book filled with riders and letters.[[56]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_9-57)
###  _Load_ , _Reload_ , Napster controversy and Newsted's departure
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/41/James_Hetfield_-_Cardiff_1996.jpg/250px-James_Hetfield_-_Cardiff_1996.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:James_Hetfield_-_Cardiff_1996.jpg)James Hetfield performing with the band during its tour for _Load_ in 1996
After almost three years of touring to promote _Metallica_ , including a headlining performance at [Woodstock '94](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Woodstock_%2794 "Woodstock '94"), Metallica returned to the studio to write and record its sixth studio album. The band went on a brief hiatus in the summer of 1995 and played a short tour, Escape from the Studio '95, which comprised three outdoor shows, including a headline show at [Donington Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donington_Park "Donington Park") supported by [Slayer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slayer "Slayer"), [Skid Row](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skid_Row_\(American_band\) "Skid Row \(American band\)"), [Slash's Snakepit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slash%27s_Snakepit "Slash's Snakepit"), [Therapy?](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Therapy%3F "Therapy?") and [Corrosion of Conformity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corrosion_of_Conformity "Corrosion of Conformity"). The band spent about a year writing and recording new songs, resulting in the release of _[Load](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Load_\(album\) "Load \(album\)")_ in 1996. _Load_ debuted at number one on the _Billboard_ 200 and [ARIA Charts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ARIA_Charts "ARIA Charts"); it was the band's second number one album.[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Charts-31) The cover art, _Blood and Semen III_ , was created by [Andres Serrano](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andres_Serrano "Andres Serrano"), who pressed a mixture of his own semen and blood between sheets of plexiglass.[[57]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_10-58) The release saw another change in the band's musical direction and a new image, which included a revamped logo and the band members having their hair cut. While _Load_ was a commercial success, the band's change in style had polarized their fanbase.[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-59) Metallica headlined the alternative rock festival [Lollapalooza](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lollapalooza "Lollapalooza") festival in mid-1996.[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Playboy-60)[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Load_review-61)
During the early production of _Load_ , the band had recorded enough material to fill a double album. It was decided that half of the songs were to be released; the band would continue to work on the remaining songs and release them the following year. This resulted in follow-up album, titled _[Reload](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reload_\(Metallica_album\) "Reload \(Metallica album\)")_. The cover art was again created by Serrano, this time using a mixture of blood and urine.[[57]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_10-58) _Reload_ , too, debuted at number one on the _Billboard_ 200 and reached number two on the Top Canadian Album chart.[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Charts-31) Hetfield said in the 2004 documentary film _[Metallica: Some Kind of Monster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica:_Some_Kind_of_Monster "Metallica: Some Kind of Monster")_ that the band initially thought some of the songs on these albums were of average quality; these were "polished and reworked" until judged releasable.[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Some_Kind_of_Monster_film-62) To promote _Reload_ , Metallica performed "[Fuel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fuel_\(Metallica_song\) "Fuel \(Metallica song\)")" and "[The Memory Remains](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Memory_Remains "The Memory Remains")" with [Marianne Faithfull](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marianne_Faithfull "Marianne Faithfull") on [NBC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NBC "NBC")'s _Saturday Night Live_ in December 1997.[[62]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_11-63)
In 1998, Metallica compiled a double album of cover songs, titled _[Garage Inc.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Garage_Inc. "Garage Inc.")_ The first disc contained newly recorded covers of songs by [Diamond Head](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diamond_Head_\(English_band\) "Diamond Head \(English band\)"), [Killing Joke](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killing_Joke "Killing Joke"), the [Misfits](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Misfits_\(band\) "Misfits \(band\)"), [Thin Lizzy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thin_Lizzy "Thin Lizzy"), [Mercyful Fate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercyful_Fate "Mercyful Fate"), [Black Sabbath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_Sabbath "Black Sabbath") and others, and the second disc featured the original version of _The $5.98 E.P. – Garage Days Re-Revisited_ , which had become a scarce collector's item. The album entered the _Billboard_ 200 at number two.[[62]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_11-63)[[63]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-64)
On April 21 and 22, 1999, Metallica recorded two performances with the [San Francisco Symphony](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco_Symphony "San Francisco Symphony"), which was conducted by [Michael Kamen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Kamen "Michael Kamen"), who had previously worked with producer Rock on "[Nothing Else Matters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nothing_Else_Matters "Nothing Else Matters")". Kamen approached Metallica in 1991 with the idea of pairing the band's music with a symphony orchestra. Kamen and his staff of over 100 composed additional orchestral material for Metallica songs. Metallica wrote two new Kamen-scored songs for the event: "[No Leaf Clover](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/No_Leaf_Clover "No Leaf Clover")" and "-Human". The audio recording and concert footage were released in 1999 as the album and concert film _[S&M](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S%26M_\(album\) "S&M \(album\)")_. It entered the _Billboard_ 200 at number two and the Australian ARIA charts and Top Internet Albums chart at number one.[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Charts-31)
In 2000, Metallica discovered that a demo of its song "[I Disappear](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I_Disappear "I Disappear")", which was supposed to be released in combination with the [_Mission: Impossible II_ soundtrack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_II_\(soundtrack\) "Mission: Impossible II \(soundtrack\)"), was receiving radio airplay. Tracing the source of the leak, the band found the file on the [Napster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Napster "Napster") [peer-to-peer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peer-to-peer "Peer-to-peer") file-sharing network and also found that the band's entire catalogue was freely available.[[64]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Testimony-65) Metallica [filed a lawsuit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica_v._Napster,_Inc. "Metallica v. Napster, Inc.") at the [U.S. District Court](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._District_Court "U.S. District Court"), [Central District of California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_District_of_California "Central District of California"), alleging that Napster violated three areas of the law: [copyright infringement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Copyright_infringement "Copyright infringement"), unlawful use of digital audio interface device and the [Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Racketeer_Influenced_and_Corrupt_Organizations_Act "Racketeer Influenced and Corrupt Organizations Act") (RICO).[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-66)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/db/Lars_Ulrich_The_Rock_Copenhagen.JPG/250px-Lars_Ulrich_The_Rock_Copenhagen.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Lars_Ulrich_The_Rock_Copenhagen.JPG)Lars Ulrich led the case against Napster for Metallica.
Ulrich provided a statement to the [Senate Judiciary Committee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Senate_Judiciary_Committee "Senate Judiciary Committee") regarding copyright infringement on July 11, 2000.[[64]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Testimony-65) [Federal Judge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Judge "Federal Judge") [Marilyn Hall Patel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marilyn_Hall_Patel "Marilyn Hall Patel") ordered the site to place a [filter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Content-control_software "Content-control software") on the program within 72 hours or be shut down.[[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-67) A settlement between Metallica and Napster was reached when German media conglomerate [Bertelsmann](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bertelsmann "Bertelsmann") BMG showed interest in purchasing the rights to Napster for $94 million. Under the terms of settlement, Napster agreed to block users who shared music by artists who do not want their music shared.[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-68) On June 3, 2002, Napster filed for [Chapter 11](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chapter_11 "Chapter 11") protection under U.S. bankruptcy laws. On September 3, 2002, an American bankruptcy judge blocked the sale of Napster to Bertelsmann and forced Napster to liquidate its assets, according to [Chapter 7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chapter_7,_Title_11,_United_States_Code "Chapter 7, Title 11, United States Code") of the U.S. bankruptcy laws.[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-69)
At the [2000 MTV Video Music Awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2000_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2000 MTV Video Music Awards"), Ulrich appeared with host [Marlon Wayans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marlon_Wayans "Marlon Wayans") in a skit that criticized the idea of using Napster to share music. Wayans played a college student listening to Metallica's "I Disappear". Ulrich walked in and asked for an explanation, and he responded to Wayans' excuse that using Napster was just "sharing" by saying that Wayans' idea of sharing was "borrowing things that were not yours without asking." He called in the Metallica road crew, who proceeded to confiscate all of Wayans' belongings, leaving him almost naked in an empty room. Napster creator [Shawn Fanning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shawn_Fanning "Shawn Fanning") responded later in the ceremony by presenting an award wearing a Metallica shirt that read: "I borrowed this shirt from a friend. Maybe, if I like it, I'll buy one of my own." Ulrich was later booed on stage at the award show when he introduced the final musical act, [Blink-182](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blink-182 "Blink-182").[[69]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-70)[[70]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-71)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/JunoAwards2009-BobRock_%28cropped%29_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-JunoAwards2009-BobRock_%28cropped%29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:JunoAwards2009-BobRock_\(cropped\)_\(cropped\).jpg)Longtime producer Bob Rock recorded bass for _St. Anger_ following Jason Newsted's departure in 2001.
Newsted left Metallica on January 17, 2001, just as plans were being made to enter the recording studio. He said he left the band for "private and personal reasons, and the physical damage I have done to myself over the years while playing the music that I love."[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-72) During a _[Playboy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Playboy "Playboy")_ interview with Metallica, Newsted said he wanted to release an album with his [side project](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Side_project "Side project"), [Echobrain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Echobrain "Echobrain").
