Hetfield was opposed to the idea and said, "When someone does a side project, it takes away from the strength of Metallica" and that a side project is "like cheating on your wife in a way."[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Playboy-60) Newsted said Hetfield had recorded vocals for a song used in the film _[South Park: Bigger, Longer& Uncut](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Park:_Bigger,_Longer_%26_Uncut "South Park: Bigger, Longer & Uncut")_, and appeared on one [Corrosion of Conformity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corrosion_of_Conformity "Corrosion of Conformity") album, [Wiseblood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wiseblood_\(Corrosion_of_Conformity_album\) "Wiseblood \(Corrosion of Conformity album\)"). Hetfield replied: "My name isn't on those records. And I'm not out trying to sell them" and raised questions such as, "Where would it end? Does he start touring with it? Does he sell shirts? Is it his band?"[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Playboy-60)
###  _Some Kind of Monster_ , _St. Anger_ , and Trujillo joins
In April 2001, filmmakers [Joe Berlinger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Berlinger "Joe Berlinger") and [Bruce Sinofsky](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bruce_Sinofsky "Bruce Sinofsky") began following Metallica to document the recording process of the band's next studio album. Over two years they recorded more than 1,000 hours of footage. On July 19, 2001, before preparations to enter the recording studio, Hetfield entered [rehab](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drug_rehabilitation "Drug rehabilitation") to treat his "alcoholism and other addictions". All recording plans were put on hold and the band's future was in doubt.[[72]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_12-73) Hetfield left rehab on December 4, 2001,[[72]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Timeline_12-73) and the band returned to the recording studio on April 12, 2002. Hetfield was required to limit his work to four hours a day between noon and 4 pm, and to spend the rest of his time with his family.[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Some_Kind_of_Monster_film-62) The footage recorded by Berlinger and Sinofsky was compiled into the documentary _[Metallica: Some Kind of Monster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica:_Some_Kind_of_Monster "Metallica: Some Kind of Monster")_ , which premiered at the [Sundance Film Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival") in January 2004. In the documentary, Newsted said his former bandmates' decision to hire a therapist to help solve their problems which he felt they could have solved on their own was "really fucking lame and weak".[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Some_Kind_of_Monster_film-62)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/eb/Robert_Trujillo_live_in_London_2008-09-15.jpg/250px-Robert_Trujillo_live_in_London_2008-09-15.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Robert_Trujillo_live_in_London_2008-09-15.jpg)Robert Trujillo joined Metallica in 2003 after the recording of _[St. Anger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Anger "St. Anger")_.
In June 2003, Metallica's eighth studio album, _[St. Anger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Anger "St. Anger")_ , debuted at number one on the _Billboard_ 200, and drew mixed reactions from critics. Ulrich's "steely" sounding snare drum and the absence of guitar solos received particular criticism.[[73]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Metacritic-74) Kevin Forest Moreau of _Shakingthrough.net_ said, "the guitars stumble in a monotone of mid-level, processed rattle; the drums don't propel as much as struggle to disguise an all-too-turgid pace; and the rage is both unfocused and leavened with too much narcissistic navel-gazing".[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-75) Brent DiCrescenzo of _[Pitchfork](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pitchfork_\(website\) "Pitchfork \(website\)")_ described it as "an utter mess".[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-76) However, _[Blender](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blender_\(magazine\) "Blender \(magazine\)")_ magazine called it the "grimiest and grimmest of the band's Bob Rock productions", and _[New York Magazine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_Magazine "New York Magazine")_ called it "utterly raw and rocking".[[76]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-St_Anger_by_Metallica-77) The title track, "[St. Anger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Anger_\(song\) "St. Anger \(song\)")", won the [Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Metal_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance") in 2004; it was used as the official theme song for [WWE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WWE "WWE")'s _[SummerSlam 2003](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SummerSlam_2003 "SummerSlam 2003")_.[[77]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-78)
For the duration of _St. Anger_ 's recording period, producer Bob Rock played bass on the album and in several live shows at which Metallica performed during that time.[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Some_Kind_of_Monster_film-62) Once the record was completed, the band started to hold auditions for Newsted's permanent replacement. Bassists [Pepper Keenan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pepper_Keenan "Pepper Keenan"), [Jeordie White](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jeordie_White "Jeordie White"), [Scott Reeder](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scott_Reeder_\(bassist\) "Scott Reeder \(bassist\)"), [Eric Avery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eric_Avery "Eric Avery"), [Danny Lohner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Danny_Lohner "Danny Lohner"), and [Chris Wyse](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chris_Wyse "Chris Wyse")—among others—auditioned for the role. After three months of auditions, [Robert Trujillo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Trujillo "Robert Trujillo"), formerly of [Suicidal Tendencies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suicidal_Tendencies "Suicidal Tendencies") and Ozzy Osbourne's band, was chosen as the new bassist.[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Some_Kind_of_Monster_film-62) Newsted, who had joined Canadian thrash metal band [Voivod](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voivod_\(band\) "Voivod \(band\)") by that time, was Trujillo's replacement in Osbourne's band during the 2003 [Ozzfest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ozzfest "Ozzfest") tour, which included Voivod.[[78]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-79)
Before the band's set at the 2004 [Download Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Download_Festival "Download Festival"), Ulrich was rushed to the hospital after having an anxiety seizure and was unable to perform.[[79]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-80) Hetfield searched for last-minute volunteers to replace Ulrich. Slayer drummer [Dave Lombardo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dave_Lombardo "Dave Lombardo") and [Slipknot](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slipknot_\(band\) "Slipknot \(band\)") drummer [Joey Jordison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joey_Jordison "Joey Jordison") volunteered. Lombardo performed "Battery" and "The Four Horsemen", Ulrich's drum technician Flemming Larsen performed "Fade to Black", and Jordison performed the remainder of the set.[[80]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-81) Having toured for two years in support of _St. Anger_ on the Summer Sanitarium Tour 2003 and the [Madly in Anger with the World Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madly_in_Anger_with_the_World_Tour "Madly in Anger with the World Tour"), with multi-platinum rock band [Godsmack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Godsmack "Godsmack") in support, Metallica took a break from performing and spent most of 2005 with friends and family. The band opened for [the Rolling Stones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Rolling_Stones "The Rolling Stones") at [SBC Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SBC_Park "SBC Park") in San Francisco on November 13 and 15, 2005.[[81]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-82)
###  _Death Magnetic_ and Rock and Roll Hall of Fame induction
In February 2006, Metallica announced on its official website that after 15 years, long-time producer Bob Rock would not be producing the band's next studio album. Instead, the band chose to work with producer [Rick Rubin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rick_Rubin "Rick Rubin").[[82]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-83) Around the same time, a petition signed by 1,500 fans was posted online in an attempt to encourage the band to prohibit Rock from producing Metallica albums, saying he had too much influence on the band's sound and musical direction. Rock said the petition hurt his children's feelings; he said, "sometimes, even with a great coach, a team keeps losing. You have to get new blood in there."[[83]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-84)
In December 2006, Metallica released a DVD titled _[The Videos 1989–2004](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Videos_1989%E2%80%932004 "The Videos 1989–2004")_ , which sold 28,000 copies in its first week and entered the _Billboard_ Top Videos chart at number three.[[84]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-85) Metallica recorded a guitar-based interpretation of [Ennio Morricone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ennio_Morricone "Ennio Morricone")'s "[The Ecstasy of Gold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Ecstasy_of_Gold "The Ecstasy of Gold")" for a tribute album titled _[We All Love Ennio Morricone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/We_All_Love_Ennio_Morricone "We All Love Ennio Morricone")_ , which was released in February 2007. The track received a Grammy nomination at the [50th Grammy Awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/50th_Grammy_Awards "50th Grammy Awards") for the category "[Best Rock Instrumental Performance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Best_Rock_Instrumental_Performance "Best Rock Instrumental Performance")".[[85]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-86) A recording of "The Ecstasy of Gold" has been played to introduce Metallica's performances since the 1980s.[[86]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-87)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/Metallica_London_2008-09-15_Kirk_and_James.jpg/250px-Metallica_London_2008-09-15_Kirk_and_James.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_London_2008-09-15_Kirk_and_James.jpg)Metallica performing in London in 2008
Metallica scheduled the release of the album _[Death Magnetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Death_Magnetic "Death Magnetic")_ as September 12, 2008, and the band filmed a music video for the album's first single, "[The Day That Never Comes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Day_That_Never_Comes "The Day That Never Comes")". On September 2, 2008, a record store in France began selling copies of _Death Magnetic_ nearly two weeks before its scheduled worldwide release date,[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-88) which resulted in the album being made available on peer-to-peer clients. This prompted the band's UK distributor [Vertigo Records](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vertigo_Records "Vertigo Records") to officially release the album on September 10, 2008. Rumors of Metallica or Warner Bros. taking legal action against the French retailer were unconfirmed, though drummer Lars Ulrich responded to the [leak](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Music_leak "Music leak") by saying, "...[[88]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-89) and, "By 2008 standards, that's a victory. If you'd told me six months ago that our record wouldn't leak until 10 days out, I would have signed up for that."[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-90)
_Death Magnetic_ marked a return to the band's traditional thrash metal sound. The album debuted at number one in the U.S., selling 490,000 units; Metallica became the first band to have five consecutive studio albums debut at number one in the history of the _Billboard_ 200.[[90]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-91) A week after its release, _Death Magnetic_ remained at number one on the _Billboard_ 200 and the European album chart; it also became the fastest selling album of 2008 in Australia.[[91]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-92) _Death Magnetic_ remained at number one on the _Billboard_ 200 album chart for three consecutive weeks. Metallica was one of two artists whose album—the other being [Jack Johnson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_Johnson_\(musician\) "Jack Johnson \(musician\)")'s album _[Sleep Through the Static](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sleep_Through_the_Static "Sleep Through the Static")_ —remained on the _Billboard_ 200 for three consecutive weeks at number one in 2008. _Death Magnetic_ also remained at number one on _Billboard_ 's Hard Rock, Modern Rock/Alternative and Rock album charts for five consecutive weeks. The album reached number one in 32 countries outside the U.S., including the UK, Canada, and Australia.[[92]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-93)[[93]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-94) In November 2008, Metallica's record deal with Warner Bros. ended and the band considered releasing its next album through the internet.[[94]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-95)[[95]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-96)
On January 14, 2009, it was announced that Metallica would be inducted into the [Rock and Roll Hall of Fame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock_and_Roll_Hall_of_Fame "Rock and Roll Hall of Fame") on April 4, 2009,[[96]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-97) and that former bassist Jason Newsted—who left the band in 2001—would perform with the band at the ceremony.[[97]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-98) Initially, it was announced that the matter had been discussed and that bassist Trujillo had agreed not to play because he "wanted to see the Black Album band".[[98]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-99) However, during the band's set of "Master of Puppets" and "Enter Sandman", both Trujillo and Newsted were on stage.[[99]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-100) Ray Burton, father of the late Cliff Burton, accepted the honor on his behalf. Although he was not to be inducted with them, Metallica invited Dave Mustaine to take part in the induction ceremony. Mustaine declined because of his touring commitments in Europe.[[100]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-101)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f8/Metallica_at_the_O2_Arena%2C_March_28th_2009.jpg/250px-Metallica_at_the_O2_Arena%2C_March_28th_2009.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_at_the_O2_Arena,_March_28th_2009.jpg)Metallica performing in London in 2009
Metallica, Slayer, Megadeth, and Anthrax performed on the same bill for the first time on June 16, 2010, at [Warsaw Babice Airport](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Warsaw_Babice_Airport "Warsaw Babice Airport"), Poland, as a part of the [Sonisphere Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sonisphere_Festival "Sonisphere Festival") series. The show in Sofia, Bulgaria, on June 22, 2010, was broadcast via satellite to cinemas.[[101]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-102) The bands also played concerts in [Bucharest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest") on June 26, 2010, and [Istanbul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul") on June 27, 2010. On June 28, 2010, _Death Magnetic_ was certified double platinum by the [RIAA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RIAA "RIAA").[[102]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-103) Metallica's [World Magnetic Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Magnetic_Tour "World Magnetic Tour") ended in [Melbourne](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne") on November 21, 2010. The band had been touring for more than two years in support of _Death Magnetic_. To accompany the final tour dates in Australia and New Zealand, a live, limited edition EP of past performances in Australia called _[Six Feet Down Under](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Six_Feet_Down_Under "Six Feet Down Under")_ was released.[[103]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-104) The EP was followed by _[Six Feet Down Under (Part II)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Six_Feet_Down_Under_\(Part_II\) "Six Feet Down Under \(Part II\)")_ , which was released on November 12, 2010.[[104]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-105) Part 2 contains a further eight songs recorded during the first two Oceanic Legs of the World Magnetic Tour. On November 26, 2010, Metallica released a live EP titled _[Live at Grimey's](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Live_at_Grimey%27s "Live at Grimey's")_ , which was recorded in June 2008 at Grimey's Record Store, just before the band's appearance at [Bonnaroo Music Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bonnaroo_Music_Festival "Bonnaroo Music Festival") that year.[[105]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-106)[[106]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-107)
In a June 2009 interview with Italy's Rock TV, Ulrich said Metallica was planning to continue touring until August 2010, and that there were no plans for a tenth album. He said he was sure the band would collaborate with producer Rick Rubin again.[[107]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-108) According to [Blabbermouth.net](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blabbermouth.net "Blabbermouth.net"), the band was considering recording its next album in the second half of 2011.[[108]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-109) In November 2010, during an interview with The Pulse of Radio, Ulrich said Metallica would return to writing in 2011. Ulrich said, "There's a bunch of balls in the air for 2011, but I think the main one is we really want to get back to writing again. We haven't really written since, what, '06, '07, and we want to get back to kind of just being creative again. Right now we are going to just chill out and then probably start up again in, I'd say, March or April, and start probably putting the creative cap back on and start writing some songs."[[109]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-110)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/39/Metallica3425.jpg/250px-Metallica3425.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica3425.jpg)Metallica performing in Sacramento in 2009
On November 9, 2010, Metallica announced it would be headlining the [Rock in Rio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock_in_Rio "Rock in Rio") festival in [Rio de Janeiro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro") on September 25, 2011.[[110]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-111)[[111]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-112) On December 13, 2010, the band announced it would again play as part of the "big four" during the Sonisphere Festival at [Knebworth House](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Knebworth_House "Knebworth House"), Hertfordshire, on July 8, 2011. It was the first time all of the "big four" members played on the same stage in the UK.[[112]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-113) On December 17, 2010, Another "big four" Sonisphere performance that would take place in France on July 9 was announced.[[113]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-114) On January 25, 2011, another "big four" performance on April 23, 2011, at the Empire Polo Club in [Indio, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indio,_California "Indio, California"), was announced. It was the first time all of the "big four" members played on the same stage in the U.S.[[114]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-115) On February 17, 2011, a show in [Gelsenkirchen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gelsenkirchen "Gelsenkirchen"), Germany, on July 2, 2011, was announced.[[115]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-116) On February 22, a "big four" show in [Milan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milan "Milan") on July 6, 2011, was announced.[[116]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-117) On March 2, 2011, another "big four" concert, which took place in [Gothenburg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gothenburg "Gothenburg") on July 3, 2011, was announced.[[117]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-118) The final "big four" concert was in New York City, at Yankee Stadium, on September 14, 2011.[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-119)
In an interview at the April 2011 Big Four concert, Robert Trujillo said Metallica will work with Rick Rubin again as producer for the new album and were "really excited to write some new music. There's no shortage of riffage in Metallica world right now." He added, "The first album with Rick was also the first album for me, so in a lot of ways, you're kind of testing the water. Now that we're comfortable with Rick and his incredible engineer, [Greg Fidelman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greg_Fidelman "Greg Fidelman"), who worked with Slayer, actually, on this last record—it's my hero—it's a great team. And it's only gonna better; I really believe that. So I'm super-excited."[[119]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-120) In June 2011, Rubin said Metallica had begun writing its new album.[[120]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-121)
On June 15, 2011, Metallica announced that recording sessions with singer-songwriter [Lou Reed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lou_Reed "Lou Reed") had concluded. The album, which was titled _[Lulu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lulu_\(Lou_Reed_and_Metallica_album\) "Lulu \(Lou Reed and Metallica album\)")_ , was recorded over several months and comprised ten songs[[121]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-122) based on [Frank Wedekind](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frank_Wedekind "Frank Wedekind")'s "Lulu" plays _[Earth Spirit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earth_Spirit_\(play\) "Earth Spirit \(play\)")_ and _[Pandora's Box](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pandora%27s_Box_\(play\) "Pandora's Box \(play\)")_.[[122]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-exclaim-123) The album was released on October 31, 2011. The recording of the album was problematic at times; Lars Ulrich later said Lou Reed challenged him to a "street fight".[[123]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-124) On October 16, 2011, Robert Trujillo confirmed that the band was back in the studio and writing new material. He said, "The writing process for the new Metallica album has begun. We've been in the studio with Rick Rubin, working on a couple of things, and we're going to be recording during the most of next year."[[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-125)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/da/Metallica_%286350334052%29.jpg/250px-Metallica_%286350334052%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_\(6350334052\).jpg)Metallica performing in Bangalore in 2011
Metallica was due to make its first appearance in India at the "India Rocks" concert, supporting the [2011 Indian Grand Prix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2011_Indian_Grand_Prix "2011 Indian Grand Prix").[[125]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-126) However, the concert was canceled when the venue was proven to be unsafe.[[126]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-127) Fans raided the stage during the event and the organizers were later arrested for fraud.[[127]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-128) Metallica made its Indian debut in Bangalore on October 30, 2011.[[128]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-129)[[129]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-130) On November 10, it was announced that Metallica would headline the main stage on Saturday June 9, 2012, at the Download Festival at Donington Park and that the band would play _The Black Album_ in its entirety.[[130]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-131) Metallica celebrated its 30th anniversary by playing four shows at [the Fillmore](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Fillmore "The Fillmore") in San Francisco in December 2011. The shows were exclusive to Met Club members and tickets were charged at $6 each or $19.81 for all four nights. The shows consisted of songs from the band's career and featured guest appearances by artists who had either helped or had influenced Metallica. These shows were notable because Lloyd Grant, Dave Mustaine, Jason Newsted, [Glenn Danzig](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glenn_Danzig "Glenn Danzig"), [Ozzy Osbourne](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ozzy_Osbourne "Ozzy Osbourne"), [Jerry Cantrell](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jerry_Cantrell "Jerry Cantrell"), [Apocalyptica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apocalyptica "Apocalyptica"), members of [Diamond Head](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diamond_Head_\(British_band\) "Diamond Head \(British band\)"), and [King Diamond](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/King_Diamond "King Diamond") joined Metallica on stage for all appropriate songs.[[131]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-132)[[132]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-133) In December 2011, Metallica began releasing songs that were written for _Death Magnetic_ but were not included on the album online.[[133]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-134) On December 13, 2011, the band released _[Beyond Magnetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beyond_Magnetic "Beyond Magnetic")_ , a digital EP release exclusively on [iTunes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ITunes "ITunes").[[134]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-135) It was released on CD in January 2012.[[135]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-136)
On February 7, 2012, Metallica announced that it would start a new music festival called [Orion Music + More](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orion_Music_%2B_More "Orion Music + More"), which took place on June 23 and 24, 2012, in Atlantic City. Metallica also confirmed that it would headline the festival on both days and would perform two of its most critically acclaimed albums in their entirety: _The Black Album_ on one night, and _Ride the Lightning_ on the other.[[136]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-137) In a July 2012 interview with Canadian radio station [99.3 The Fox](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/99.3_The_Fox "99.3 The Fox"), Ulrich said Metallica would not release its new album until at least early 2014.[[137]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-newalbum-138)
[independent record label](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Independent_record_label "Independent record label"), **Blackened Recordings** , which will produce the band's future releases.[[138]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-139)[[139]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-140) The band acquired the rights to all of its studio albums, which were all reissued through the new label. Blackened releases were licensed through Warner subsidiary [Rhino Entertainment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rhino_Entertainment "Rhino Entertainment") in North America and internationally through [Universal Music](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universal_Music "Universal Music").[[140]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-LATimes-141)[[141]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-142) On September 20, 2012, Metallica announced via its official website that a new DVD containing footage of shows it performed in Quebec in 2009 would be released that December; fans would get the chance to vote for two setlists that would appear on the DVD.[[142]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-143) The film, titled _[Quebec Magnetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quebec_Magnetic "Quebec Magnetic")_ , was released in the U.S. on December 10, 2012.[[143]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-144)
###  _Metallica: Through the Never_ and _Hardwired... to Self-Destruct_
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/Metallica_2013.jpg/250px-Metallica_2013.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_2013.jpg)Metallica at [San Diego Comic-Con](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con") in 2013
In an interview with _[Classic Rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classic_Rock_\(magazine\) "Classic Rock \(magazine\)")_ on January 8, 2013, Ulrich said regarding the band's upcoming album, "What we're doing now certainly sounds like a continuation [of _Death Magnetic_]". He also said, "I love Rick [Rubin]. We all love Rick. We're in touch with Rick constantly. We'll see where it goes. It would stun me if the record came out in 2013."[[144]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-145) Also in 2013, the band starred in a 3D concert film titled _[Metallica: Through the Never](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica:_Through_the_Never "Metallica: Through the Never")_ , which was directed by [Antal Nimród](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antal_Nimr%C3%B3d "Antal Nimród") and was released in [IMAX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IMAX "IMAX") theaters on September 27.[[145]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-146)
In an interview dated July 22, 2013, Ulrich told _Ultimate Guitar_ , "2014 will be all about making a new Metallica record"; he said the album will most likely be released during 2015.[[146]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-147) Kirk Hammett and Robert Trujillo later confirmed the band's intention to enter the studio.[[147]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-148) At the second Orion Music + More festival held in Detroit, the band played under the name "Dehaan"—a reference to actor [Dane DeHaan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dane_DeHaan "Dane DeHaan"), who starred in _Metallica: Through the Never_.[[148]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-149) The band performed its debut album _Kill 'Em All_ in its entirety, celebrating the 30th anniversary of its release.[[149]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-150) On December 8, 2013, the band played a show called "[Freeze 'Em All](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Freeze_%27Em_All "Freeze 'Em All")" in Antarctica, becoming the first band to play on all seven continents.[[150]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-151) The performance was filmed and released as a live album the same month.[[151]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-152)
At the [56th Annual Grammy Awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/56th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "56th Annual Grammy Awards") in January 2014, Metallica performed "One" with Chinese pianist [Lang Lang](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lang_Lang "Lang Lang").[[152]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-153) In March 2014, Metallica began a tour called "Metallica By Request", in which fans request songs for the band to perform.[[153]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-154) A new song, titled "Lords of Summer" was written for the concerts and released as a "first take" demo in June 2014.[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-155) In June 2014, the band headlined the [Glastonbury Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glastonbury_Festival "Glastonbury Festival") in an attempt to attract new fans. Ulrich said, "We have one shot, you never know if you'll be invited back".[[155]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-156) In November 2014, Metallica performed at the closing ceremony of [BlizzCon 2014](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BlizzCon "BlizzCon").[[156]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-157)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/Metallica_-_The_O2_-_Tuesday_24th_October_2017_MetallicaO2241017-3_%2837955387006%29_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Metallica_-_The_O2_-_Tuesday_24th_October_2017_MetallicaO2241017-3_%2837955387006%29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_-_The_O2_-_Tuesday_24th_October_2017_MetallicaO2241017-3_\(37955387006\)_\(cropped\).jpg)Metallica performing in London in 2017
In January 2015, Metallica announced a "Metallica Night" with the [San Jose Sharks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Jose_Sharks "San Jose Sharks"), which featured a Q&A session with the band and a charity auction benefiting the San Francisco Bay Chapter of the Sierra Club, but no performances.[[157]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-158) They were announced to headline Lollapalooza in March 2015, returning to perform there for the first time in 20 years.[[158]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-159) On May 2, 2015, Metallica performed their third annual Metallica Day at [AT&T Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AT%26T_Park "AT&T Park").[[159]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-160) Metallica were also announced to play at [X Games](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X_Games "X Games") for the first time at [X Games Austin 2015](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X_Games_Austin_2015 "X Games Austin 2015") in Austin, Texas.[[160]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-161) On June 14, 2015, Hetfield and Hammett performed "[The Star-Spangled Banner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Star-Spangled_Banner "The Star-Spangled Banner")" live via electric guitars prior to game 5 of the [NBA Finals](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2015_NBA_Finals "2015 NBA Finals") between the [Cleveland Cavaliers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Cleveland_Cavaliers_season "2014–15 Cleveland Cavaliers season") and [Golden State Warriors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Golden_State_Warriors_season "2014–15 Golden State Warriors season") at [Oracle Arena](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oracle_Arena "Oracle Arena") in [Oakland, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oakland,_California "Oakland, California").[[161]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-162)[[162]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-163)[[163]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-164) In late October, the band unveiled a new website with an introduction from Ulrich containing footage from the studio of the band working on new material.[[164]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-165) On November 2, Metallica were announced to play "The Night Before" [Super Bowl 50](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Super_Bowl_50 "Super Bowl 50") at AT&T Park.[[165]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-166) Metallica announced they would be opening the [U.S. Bank Stadium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._Bank_Stadium "U.S. Bank Stadium") on August 20, 2016, with [Avenged Sevenfold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avenged_Sevenfold "Avenged Sevenfold") and [Volbeat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Volbeat "Volbeat") as support.[[166]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-167)
In April 2016, during the week leading up to [Record Store Day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Record_Store_Day "Record Store Day"), for which the band was its ambassador for 2016, Ulrich told _[Billboard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Billboard_\(magazine\) "Billboard \(magazine\)")_ that the band's expanded role within the music industry had played a part in the amount of time that it had taken to write and record the album. "The way we do things now is very different than the way we did things back in the days of _Kill 'Em All_ and _Ride the Lightning_. Nowadays we like to do so many different things." Ulrich was also optimistic that production of the album had almost reached its completion. "Unless something radical happens it would be difficult for me to believe that it won't come out in 2016".[[167]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-168) On August 18, 2016, the band announced via their website that their tenth studio album, _[Hardwired... to Self-Destruct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hardwired..._to_Self-Destruct "Hardwired... to Self-Destruct")_ , would be released worldwide on November 18, 2016, via their independent label, Blackened Recordings. They also unveiled the track listing, album artwork, and released a music video for the album's first single, "Hardwired".[[168]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-169) The album was released as scheduled[[169]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-170) and debuted at number one on the [_Billboard_ 200](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200").[[170]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Billboard_200_debut-171)
Metallica announced they would be touring the US and Canada in summer of 2017 for the [WorldWired Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/WorldWired_Tour "WorldWired Tour"). The stadium tour also includes Avenged Sevenfold, Volbeat and [Gojira](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gojira_\(band\) "Gojira \(band\)") as supporting acts.[[171]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-172) On August 7, 2017, Metallica was invited by the [San Francisco Giants](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Francisco_Giants "San Francisco Giants") again for the fifth annual "Metallica Night" with Hammett and Hetfield performing the national anthem.[[172]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-173) In January 2018, the band announced that they would be reissuing _The $5.98 E.P. – Garage Days Re-Revisited_ on April 13 for [Record Store Day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Record_Store_Day "Record Store Day"),[[173]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-174) and the sixth annual "Metallica Night" was also announced a few weeks later, this time in April, with all proceeds going to the All Within My Hands Foundation, which the band created in late 2017.[[174]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-175) In February 2018, the band announced a second set of North American tour dates, most of which for cities that they had not visited in up to thirty years.[[175]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-176)
###  _S &M2_ and _72 Seasons_
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/ba/Metallica_at_Ippodromo_San_Siro%2C_Milan%2C_May_8th%2C_2019.jpg/250px-Metallica_at_Ippodromo_San_Siro%2C_Milan%2C_May_8th%2C_2019.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_at_Ippodromo_San_Siro,_Milan,_May_8th,_2019.jpg)Metallica performing in Milan, 2019
In an interview with Australian magazine _[The Music](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Music_\(magazine\) "The Music \(magazine\)")_ 's official podcast in March 2019, Trujillo said that Metallica had begun jamming on new material for its next studio album. "I'm excited about the next record because I believe it will also be a culmination of the two [previous] records and another journey. There's no shortage of original ideas, that's the beauty of being in this band." He estimated that the album would be released sooner than the previous two did, saying: "this time around I think we'll be able to jump on it a lot quicker and jump in the studio and start working."[[176]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-177) In an interview with Australian magazine _Mixdown_ the following month, Hammett said that the band had tentative plans to enter the studio after the conclusion of its WorldWired Tour. He stated, "We're in our third year since _Hardwired_. Maybe we can get a bit more focus and go into the studio a bit sooner." After not contributing any writing to _Hardwired... to Self-Destruct_ , Hammett said regarding his ideas for the new album, "I have a ton of material. I've over-compensated, so I'm ready to go anytime."[[177]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-178)
In March 2019, Metallica announced that its WorldWired Tour would continue into Australia and New Zealand in October with Slipknot in support.[[178]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-179)[[179]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-180) Later that month, the band announced that it would perform at the grand opening of San Francisco's new [Chase Center](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chase_Center "Chase Center") with the San Francisco Symphony in September to celebrate the twenty-year anniversary of _S &M_.[[180]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-181) The commemorative shows, titled _[S&M2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S%26M2 "S&M2")_, were screened in more than 3,000 theaters worldwide on October 9;[[181]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-UCR-182)[[182]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-BillboardS&M2-183) the event featured arrangements from the original _S &M_ concerts as well as new arrangements for songs recorded since then[[181]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-UCR-182)[[182]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-BillboardS&M2-183) and a cover of the [Alexander Mosolov](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Mosolov "Alexander Mosolov") piece _[Iron Foundry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iron_Foundry "Iron Foundry")_ ,[[183]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-184) and were conducted by [Edwin Outwater](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edwin_Outwater "Edwin Outwater") and San Francisco Symphony music director [Michael Tilson Thomas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Tilson_Thomas "Michael Tilson Thomas").[[184]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-185) _S &M2_ went on to earn $5.5 million at the box office, making it the biggest global rock event cinema release of all time; a second screening was later announced for October 30 as a result.[[185]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-186)[[186]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-187)[[187]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-188) In August 2020, the band announced that they would release the _S &M2_ performances as an album, DVD and box set.[[188]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-189)[[189]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-190)
In July 2019, Metallica announced a set of South American tour dates for April 2020 with [Greta Van Fleet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greta_Van_Fleet "Greta Van Fleet") in support.[[190]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-191)[[191]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-192) In September, ahead of that year's [Global Citizen Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Global_Citizen_Festival "Global Citizen Festival"), it was announced that Metallica would perform at the following year's festival in September 2020 alongside artists such as [Billie Eilish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Billie_Eilish "Billie Eilish"), [Miley Cyrus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miley_Cyrus "Miley Cyrus") and [Coldplay](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coldplay "Coldplay"), in what would be the final event of [Global Poverty Project](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Global_Poverty_Project "Global Poverty Project")'s year-long Global Goal Live: The Possible Dream campaign.[[192]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-193) The following day, on September 27, Metallica announced that Hetfield had re-entered a rehabilitation program and that its Australia/New Zealand tour would be postponed.[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-HetfieldNME-194)[[194]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-HetfieldCR-195)[[195]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Hetfieldnews-196) In a statement by Ulrich, Hammett and Trujillo, the band spoke of the devastation of the news, saying that Hetfield "[had] been struggling with addiction on and off for many years" and that all tickets would be fully refunded.[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-HetfieldNME-194)[[194]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-HetfieldCR-195)[[195]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Hetfieldnews-196) Ulrich later added that Hetfield was "in the process of healing himself", and that the band hoped to return to Australia and New Zealand in 2020.[[196]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-197) The band's other commitments, including a benefit concert in March 2020, were still expected to continue as planned;[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-HetfieldNME-194) a further five US festival appearances were announced in October.[[197]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-198) These shows were later postponed or cancelled due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") and to support Hetfield's recovery.[[198]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-199)[[199]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-200)
In March 2020, the band began a series on YouTube and Facebook called _#MetallicaMondays_ , where they streamed full archived shows every Monday to relieve boredom while staying home and social distancing amid the pandemic.[[200]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-201)[[201]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-202) In May 2020, while in quarantine, Metallica performed a virtual acoustic version of "[Blackened](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blackened_\(song\) "Blackened \(song\)")",[[202]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-203) titled "Blackened 2020", which was later made available for download.[[203]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-204) In an interview with [Marc Benioff](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marc_Benioff "Marc Benioff") in April 2020, Ulrich stated that Metallica could work on its next studio album while in quarantine.[[204]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-205) Trujillo told _The Vinyl Guide_ in June that the band was "excited about cultivating new ideas" for its new album. "We communicate every week, which is really great, so we have our connection intact. And what we've started doing is basically just really concentrating on our home studios and being creative from our homes and navigating through ideas and building on new ideas. And that's where we're at right now". He also said that the band was working towards eventually entering a studio to record the album.[[205]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-206) On August 10, 2020, Metallica played a show at Gundlach-Bundschu Winery in [Sonoma, California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sonoma,_California "Sonoma, California"), which was only attended by a few crew members, and it was recorded and played for drive-in movies across the U.S. and Canada on August 29.[[206]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-207)
In May 2021, the band announced that they would do one more _#MetallicaMondays_ on May 24 to benefit their All Within My Hands Foundation.[[207]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-hadusek-208) The concert dates to September 6, 2018, in [Lincoln, Nebraska](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lincoln,_Nebraska "Lincoln, Nebraska").[[207]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-hadusek-208) On Labor Day, September 6, 2021, the band's All Within My Hands Foundation raised $377,450. Metallica teamed up with workwear brand Carhartt on the initiative, with the clothing brand donating all of their sales proceeds on the holiday to the band's Metallica Scholars initiative, which provides opportunities to people interested in pursuing essential workforce jobs.[[208]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-209)
On November 28, 2022, the band released the single "[Lux Æterna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lux_%C3%86terna_\(Metallica_song\) "Lux Æterna \(Metallica song\)")".[[209]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-210)[[210]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-211) On January 19, 2023, Metallica released the second single titled "[Screaming Suicide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Screaming_Suicide "Screaming Suicide")".[[211]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-212)[[212]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-213) On March 1, 2023, Metallica released the third single titled "[If Darkness Had a Son](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/If_Darkness_Had_a_Son "If Darkness Had a Son")".[[213]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-214)[[214]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-215) On March 30, 2023, Metallica released a music video for the album's title track, "[72 Seasons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/72_Seasons_\(song\) "72 Seasons \(song\)")".[[215]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-216)[[216]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-217) The band released their eleventh studio album _[72 Seasons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/72_Seasons "72 Seasons")_ on April 14, 2023.[[217]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-218) Metallica promoted the album with a three-and-a-half-year-long tour, titled the [M72 World Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M72_World_Tour "M72 World Tour"), which began on April 27, 2023, in Amsterdam and is set to conclude on July 5, 2026, in London.[[218]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-219)[[219]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-220)[[220]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-221)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/14/Metallica_2025_Marvel_Stadium.jpg/250px-Metallica_2025_Marvel_Stadium.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Metallica_2025_Marvel_Stadium.jpg)Metallica performing in Melbourne in 2025
In an interview on _Loudwire Nights_ radio program in April 2023, Ulrich stated that "there [were] some ideas [Metallica] could start with" for the follow-up to _72 Seasons_ , adding, "I'm looking forward to going live and being in sharing mode for the next couple of years. The beauty of being with these guys, I kind of joke, James writes more riffs when he's tuning his guitar than most people do in a lifetime. That's the beauty of being in band with these guys and I'm eternally grateful. I'm dumbfounded by it." When asked about "leftovers" that could be used for the next Metallica album, Ulrich said, "There's always a few riffs in the riff tank."[[221]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-222) In May 2024, Hetfield confirmed that he has been "writing lots of music, playing guitar pretty much every day".[[222]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-223)
In a March 2025 interview with _[Rolling Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")_ , Hammett confirmed that he has been working on ideas for the next Metallica album: "I have 767 new ones for the next album. It is such a nightmare going through this stuff, too. And I'm the one responsible for all of it and I can't do it...I don't foresee us starting the next album for at least another year because we're still finishing the tour."[[223]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-224)
## Style and lyrical themes

Metallica played [thrash](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thrash_metal "Thrash metal")[[227]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-228) and [speed metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_metal "Speed metal")[[229]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-230) on its first four albums before changing its sound to a more traditional [heavy metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heavy_metal_music "Heavy metal music") style on its [self-titled 1991 album](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica_\(album\) "Metallica \(album\)").[[232]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-233) The band then moved towards a [hard rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hard_rock "Hard rock") sound on _[Load](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Load_\(album\) "Load \(album\)")_ (1996) and _[Reload](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reload_\(Metallica_album\) "Reload \(Metallica album\)")_ (1997).[[233]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-234) 2008's _[Death Magnetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Death_Magnetic "Death Magnetic")_ saw them returning to a more thrash-influenced sound.[[234]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-235)
The band was influenced by early heavy metal and hard rock bands and artists such as [Black Sabbath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_Sabbath "Black Sabbath"), [Pink Floyd](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pink_Floyd "Pink Floyd"), [Deep Purple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deep_Purple "Deep Purple"), [Kiss](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kiss_\(band\) "Kiss \(band\)"), [Led Zeppelin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Led_Zeppelin "Led Zeppelin"), [Queen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queen_\(band\) "Queen \(band\)"), [Ted Nugent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ted_Nugent "Ted Nugent"), [AC/DC](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AC/DC "AC/DC"), [Rush](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rush_\(band\) "Rush \(band\)"), [Aerosmith](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aerosmith "Aerosmith"), and [Judas Priest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Judas_Priest "Judas Priest"),[[235]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-guitarworld-236) [new wave of British heavy metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_wave_of_British_heavy_metal "New wave of British heavy metal") bands [Raven](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raven_\(British_band\) "Raven \(British band\)"),[[236]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-237) [Venom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venom_\(band\) "Venom \(band\)"), [Motörhead](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mot%C3%B6rhead "Motörhead"), [Saxon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_\(band\) "Saxon \(band\)"), [Diamond Head](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diamond_Head_\(English_band\) "Diamond Head \(English band\)"), [Blitzkrieg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blitzkrieg_\(metal_band\) "Blitzkrieg \(metal band\)"), and [Iron Maiden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iron_Maiden "Iron Maiden") and European bands [Scorpions](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scorpions_\(band\) "Scorpions \(band\)"),[[235]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-guitarworld-236) [Accept](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Accept_\(band\) "Accept \(band\)"),[[237]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-238) and [Mercyful Fate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercyful_Fate "Mercyful Fate").[[238]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-239) They were also influenced by [punk rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punk_rock "Punk rock") bands such as the [Ramones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramones "Ramones"), the [Sex Pistols](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sex_Pistols "Sex Pistols"), and [Misfits](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Misfits_\(band\) "Misfits \(band\)"), [post-punk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Post-punk "Post-punk") band [Killing Joke](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killing_Joke "Killing Joke")[[239]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Rolling_Stone-240) and [hardcore punk](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hardcore_punk "Hardcore punk") bands [Discharge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Discharge_\(band\) "Discharge \(band\)"),[[240]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-241) [GBH](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GBH_\(band\) "GBH \(band\)"),[[241]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-242) and [Suicidal Tendencies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suicidal_Tendencies "Suicidal Tendencies").[[242]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-243)
Lars Ulrich has cited Iron Maiden as probably the biggest influence on Metallica's career.[[243]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-244) The band's early releases contained fast tempos, harmonized leads, and nine-minute instrumental tracks. Steve Huey of AllMusic said _Ride the Lightning_ featured "extended, progressive epics; tight, concise groove-rockers".[[244]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Ride_the_Lightning_-_Metallica-245) He also said Metallica expanded its compositional technique and range of expression to take on a more aggressive approach in following releases, and lyrics dealt with personal and socially conscious issues.[[244]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Ride_the_Lightning_-_Metallica-245) Religious and military leaders, rage, insanity, monsters, and drugs—among other themes—were explored on _Master of Puppets_.[[245]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Master_of_Puppets_-_Metallica-246)
In 1991, Huey said Metallica with new producer Bob Rock simplified and streamlined its music for a more commercial approach to appeal to mainstream audiences.[[246]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Metallica_AMG-247) [Robert Palmer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Palmer_\(American_writer\) "Robert Palmer \(American writer\)") of _[Rolling Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")_ said the band abandoned its aggressive, fast tempos to expand its music and expressive [range](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Range_\(music\) "Range \(music\)").[[247]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Metallica_RS_review-248) The change in direction proved commercially successful; _Metallica_ was the band's first album to peak at number one on the _Billboard_ 200. Metallica noticed changes to the rock scene created by the [grunge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grunge "Grunge") movement of the early-1990s. In _Load_ —an album that has been described as having "an almost [alternative rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alternative_rock "Alternative rock")" approach—the band changed musical direction again and focused on non-metal influences.[[248]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Metallica_-_ReLoad-249)
Metallica's new lyrical approach moved away from drugs and monsters, and focused on anger, loss, and retribution. Some fans and critics were not pleased with this change, which included haircuts, the cover art of _Load_ , a new main logo, and headlining the [Lollapalooza](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lollapalooza "Lollapalooza") festival of 1996.[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Load_review-61) [David Fricke](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_Fricke "David Fricke") of _Rolling Stone_ described the move as "goodbye to the moldy stricture and dead-end Puritanism of no-frills thrash", and called _Load_ the heaviest record of 1996.[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Load_review-61) With the release of _ReLoad_ in 1997, the band displayed [blues](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blues "Blues") and early hard rock influences, incorporating more rhythm and harmony in song structures.[[248]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Metallica_-_ReLoad-249)
_St. Anger_ marked another large change in the band's sound. Guitar solos were excluded from the album, leaving a "raw and unpolished sound".[[76]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-St_Anger_by_Metallica-77) The band used [drop C tuning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drop_C_tuning "Drop C tuning"); Ulrich's snare drum received particular criticism. _New York Magazine_ 's Ethan Brown said it "reverberates with a thwong". The album's lyrics deal with Hetfield's drug rehabilitation and include references to the devil, anti-drug themes, claustrophobia, impending doom, and religious hypocrisy.[[249]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Music_Review_St._Anger-250)[[250]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-AMG_-_St._Anger-251) At the advice of producer Rick Rubin, for its ninth studio album _Death Magnetic_ , the band returned to [standard tuning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_tuning "Standard tuning") and guitar solos.[[251]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Metallica_Drummer_on_Producer_Rick_Rubin:_'He's_Forced_Us_to_Rethink_Big-Picture_Stuff'-252) As a return to Metallica's thrash roots, _Death Magnetic_ was a [riff](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ostinato#Riff "Ostinato")-oriented album featuring intense guitar solos and subtle lyrics about suicide and redemption.[[252]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-253)
## All Within My Hands Foundation
In February 2017, Metallica launched All Within My Hands Foundation, "dedicated to creating sustainable communities by supporting workforce education, the fight against hunger, and other critical local services". For example, the group works with various selected charities to organize volunteer projects at food banks. It also works with workforce training institutions on its Metallica Scholars grant program to help chosen students learn new trade skills.[[253]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-254) The Foundation has also donated and raised funds for [World Central Kitchen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Central_Kitchen "World Central Kitchen")'s relief efforts during the [Russo-Ukrainian war](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russo-Ukrainian_war_\(2022%E2%80%93present\) "Russo-Ukrainian war \(2022–present\)")[[254]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-255)[[255]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-256) and granted $200,000 for relief efforts after the destructive [2023 Hawaii wildfires](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2023_Hawaii_wildfires "2023 Hawaii wildfires").[[256]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-257) The band has also performed fundraising concerts for the Foundation.[[257]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-258) The Foundation takes its name from the song _[All Within My Hands](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/All_Within_My_Hands "All Within My Hands")_ from the St. Anger album. 
## Legacy and influence
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/10/Hetfield_and_Hammett_2025_Marvel_Stadium_%281%29.jpg/250px-Hetfield_and_Hammett_2025_Marvel_Stadium_%281%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Hetfield_and_Hammett_2025_Marvel_Stadium_\(1\).jpg)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/50/Ulrich_and_Trujillo_2025_Marvel_Stadium_%283%29.jpg/250px-Ulrich_and_Trujillo_2025_Marvel_Stadium_%283%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Ulrich_and_Trujillo_2025_Marvel_Stadium_\(3\).jpg)
Metallica performing in Melbourne in 2025
Metallica has become one of the most influential heavy metal bands of all time and is credited as one of the "big four" of thrash metal, along with Slayer, Anthrax, and Megadeth.[[258]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-259) The band has sold more than 125 million records worldwide,[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-sold-3) including an RIAA-certified 71 million and [Nielsen SoundScan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan")-reported 53 million in the US, making Metallica one of [the most commercially successful bands of all time](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists").[[259]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-260)[[260]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-261) The writers of _The Rolling Stone Encyclopedia of Rock & Roll_ said Metallica gave heavy metal "a much-needed charge".[[261]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-262) Stephen Thomas Erlewine and Greg Prato of Allmusic said Metallica "expanded the limits of thrash, using speed and volume not for their own sake, but to enhance their intricately structured compositions", and called the band "easily the best, most influential heavy metal band of the '80s, responsible for bringing the music back to Earth".[[262]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-263)
[Jonathan Davis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jonathan_Davis "Jonathan Davis") of [Korn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Korn "Korn") said he respects Metallica as his favorite band; he said, "I love that they've done things their own way and they've persevered over the years and they're still relevant to this day. I think they're one of the greatest bands ever."[[263]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Why_they_Rule?_Metallica-264) [Godsmack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Godsmack "Godsmack") drummer [Shannon Larkin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shannon_Larkin "Shannon Larkin") said Metallica has been the biggest influence on the band, stating, "they really changed my life when I was 16 years old—I'd never heard anything that heavy".[[264]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-265) [Chuck Billy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chuck_Billy "Chuck Billy") of [Testament](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Testament_\(band\) "Testament \(band\)") has also cited Metallica as an influence on the band, as well as Hetfield as "an inspiration with clever lyrics"; he added, "When I first started hearing Metallica it was something new to me the way his cadence of vocal styles sang to the music."[[265]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-266)[[266]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-267) Vocalist and guitarist [Robb Flynn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robb_Flynn "Robb Flynn") of [Machine Head](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_Head_\(band\) "Machine Head \(band\)") said that when creating the band's 2007 album, _[The Blackening](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Blackening "The Blackening")_ , "What we mean is an album that has the power, influence and epic grandeur of that album _Master of Puppets_ —and the staying power—a timeless record like that".[[267]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-268) [Gojira](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gojira_\(band\) "Gojira \(band\)") guitarist [Christian Andreu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christian_Andreu "Christian Andreu") said it was while listening to _Ride the Lightning_ that he started making music; saying "we find on the album 'Fade to Black', 'For Whom the Bell Tolls', emblematic pieces".[[268]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-269) [Trivium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trivium_\(band\) "Trivium \(band\)") guitarists [Corey Beaulieu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corey_Beaulieu "Corey Beaulieu") and [Matt Heafy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matt_Heafy "Matt Heafy") said that when they heard Metallica they wanted to start playing guitar.[[269]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-270)[[270]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-271) [M. Shadows](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M._Shadows "M. Shadows") of [Avenged Sevenfold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avenged_Sevenfold "Avenged Sevenfold") said touring with Metallica was the band's career highlight, and said, "Selling tons of records and playing huge shows will never compare to meeting your idols _Metallica_ ".[[271]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Avenged_Sevenfold_Interview-272) [God Forbid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/God_Forbid "God Forbid") guitarists Doc and Dallas Coyle were inspired by Metallica as they grew up, and the band's bassist John Outcalt admires Burton as a "rocker".[[272]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-273) [Ill Niño](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ill_Ni%C3%B1o "Ill Niño") drummer [Dave Chavarri](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dave_Chavarri "Dave Chavarri") said he finds early Metallica releases are "heavy, raw, rebellious. It said, 'fuck you'",[[273]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-274) and [Adema](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Adema "Adema") drummer Kris Kohls said the band is influenced by Metallica.[[274]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-275)
On April 4, 2009, Metallica were inducted into the [Rock and Roll Hall of Fame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock_and_Roll_Hall_of_Fame "Rock and Roll Hall of Fame"). They entered the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame the second year they were eligible and first year they were nominated. Metallica's induction into the Hall included its current lineup, [James Hetfield](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_Hetfield "James Hetfield"), [Kirk Hammett](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kirk_Hammett "Kirk Hammett"), [Robert Trujillo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Trujillo "Robert Trujillo"), and [Lars Ulrich](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lars_Ulrich "Lars Ulrich"), and former members [Jason Newsted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jason_Newsted "Jason Newsted") and [Cliff Burton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cliff_Burton "Cliff Burton").[[275]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-276)
[MTV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MTV "MTV") ranked Metallica the third "Greatest Heavy Metal Band in History".[[263]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-Why_they_Rule?_Metallica-264) Metallica was ranked 42nd on [VH1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/VH1 "VH1")'s "100 Greatest Artists of All Time",[[276]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-277) was listed fifth on VH1's "100 Greatest Artists of Hard Rock",[[277]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-278) and the band was number one on VH1's "20 Greatest Metal Bands" list.[[278]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-279) _[Rolling Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")_ placed the band 61st on its list of "The 100 Greatest Artists of All Time";[[279]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-280) its albums _Master of Puppets_ and _Metallica_ were ranked at numbers 167 and 252 respectively on the magazine's list of _[The 500 Greatest Albums of All Time](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_500_Greatest_Albums_of_All_Time "The 500 Greatest Albums of All Time")_.[[280]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-281) _Master of Puppets_ was named in _Q Magazine_ 's "50 Heaviest Albums of All Time",[[281]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-282) and was ranked number one on IGN's "Top 25 Metal Albums",[[282]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-283) and number one on Metal-rules.com's "Top 100 Heavy Metal Albums" list.[[283]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-284) "[Enter Sandman](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enter_Sandman "Enter Sandman")" was ranked number 399 on _Rolling Stone_ 's "500 Greatest Songs of All Time".[[284]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-285)
_[Kerrang!](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kerrang! "Kerrang!")_ released a tribute album titled _Master of Puppets: Remastered_ with the April 8, 2006, edition of the magazine to celebrate the 20th anniversary of _Master of Puppets_. The album featured cover versions of Metallica songs by Machine Head, [Bullet for My Valentine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bullet_for_My_Valentine "Bullet for My Valentine"), [Chimaira](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chimaira "Chimaira"), [Mastodon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mastodon_\(band\) "Mastodon \(band\)"), [Mendeed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mendeed "Mendeed"), and Trivium—all of which are influenced by Metallica. At least 15 Metallica tribute albums have been released. On September 10, 2006, Metallica guest starred on _[The Simpsons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")_ ' eighteenth-season premiere, "[The Mook, the Chef, the Wife and Her Homer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Mook,_the_Chef,_the_Wife_and_Her_Homer "The Mook, the Chef, the Wife and Her Homer")".[[285]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-286) Hammett's and Hetfield's voices were used in three episodes of the animated television series _[Metalocalypse](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metalocalypse "Metalocalypse")_.[[286]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-287)
They were cited as an influence by many [heavy metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heavy_metal_music "Heavy metal music") bands including [Sepultura](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sepultura
