\(band\)").[[295]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-296)
In 1996, Finnish [cello metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cello_metal "Cello metal") band Apocalyptica released a tribute album titled _[Plays Metallica by Four Cellos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plays_Metallica_by_Four_Cellos "Plays Metallica by Four Cellos")_ , which features eight Metallica songs played on cellos. A parody band named [Beatallica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beatallica "Beatallica") plays music using a combination of [the Beatles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") and Metallica songs. Beatallica faced legal troubles when [Sony](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sony "Sony"), which owns the Beatles' catalog, issued a [cease and desist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cease_and_desist "Cease and desist") order, claiming "substantial and irreparable injury" and ordering the group to pay damages. Ulrich, a fan of Beatallica, asked Metallica's lawyer Peter Paterno to help settle the case.[[296]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-297)
On March 7, 1999, Metallica was inducted into the San Francisco Walk of Fame. The mayor of San Francisco, [Willie Brown](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Willie_Lewis_Brown,_Jr. "Willie Lewis Brown, Jr."), proclaimed the day "Official Metallica Day".[[297]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-298) The band was awarded the [MTV Icon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MTV_Icon "MTV Icon") award in 2003, and a concert paying tribute to the band with artists performing its songs was held. Performances included [Sum 41](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sum_41 "Sum 41") and a medley of "For Whom the Bell Tolls", "Enter Sandman", and "Master of Puppets". [Staind](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Staind "Staind") covered "Nothing Else Matters", [Avril Lavigne](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Avril_Lavigne "Avril Lavigne") played "Fuel", hip-hop artist [Snoop Dogg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Snoop_Dogg "Snoop Dogg") performed "Sad but True", [Korn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Korn "Korn") played "[One](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One_\(Metallica_song\) "One \(Metallica song\)")", and [Limp Bizkit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limp_Bizkit "Limp Bizkit") performed "Welcome Home (Sanitarium)".[[298]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-299)
The _[Guitar Hero](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guitar_Hero "Guitar Hero")_ video game series included several of Metallica's songs. "One" was used in _[Guitar Hero III](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guitar_Hero_III "Guitar Hero III")_. The album _Death Magnetic_ was later released as purchasable, downloadable content for the game. "Trapped Under Ice" was featured in the sequel, _[Guitar Hero World Tour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guitar_Hero_World_Tour "Guitar Hero World Tour")_. In 2009, Metallica collaborated with the game's developers to make _[Guitar Hero: Metallica](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guitar_Hero:_Metallica "Guitar Hero: Metallica")_ , which included a number of Metallica's songs. [Harmonix](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harmonix "Harmonix")' video game series _[Rock Band](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock_Band "Rock Band")_ included "Enter Sandman" and "Battery"; "Ride the Lightning", "Blackened", and "...And Justice for All" were released as downloadable tracks. In 2013, due to expiring content licenses, "Ride the Lightning", "Blackened", and "...And Justice for All" are no longer available for download.[[299]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-300) _[Fortnite Festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fortnite_Festival "Fortnite Festival")_ , a 2023 rhythm game developed by Harmonix as part of _[Fortnite](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fortnite "Fortnite")_ , held a Metallica-centric season from June to August 2024 which included "One", "Lux Æterna", and "Master of Puppets". Metallica also performed virtually in _Fortnite_ in "Metallica: Fuel. Fire. Fury.", with cosmetics based on Hetfield, Ulrich, Hammett and Trujillo being added to the game.[[300]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-301)[[301]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-302)
In October 2020, [Miley Cyrus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Miley_Cyrus "Miley Cyrus") announced that she was planning on recording a Metallica covers album and on January 7, 2021, she announced that she has recorded a cover version of "[Nothing Else Matters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nothing_Else_Matters "Nothing Else Matters")" that would feature [Elton John](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John") on piano, drummer [Chad Smith](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chad_Smith "Chad Smith") from [Red Hot Chili Peppers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_Hot_Chili_Peppers "Red Hot Chili Peppers") and [cellist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cellist "Cellist") [Yo-Yo Ma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yo-Yo_Ma "Yo-Yo Ma"). This version was produced by [Andrew Watt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Watt "Andrew Watt"), and was released as a promotional single for the tribute album _[The Metallica Blacklist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Metallica_Blacklist "The Metallica Blacklist")._[[302]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-303)[[303]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-304)
In late 2024, _[Sky News](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sky_News "Sky News")_ ran an article on Gaza's first rock group, [Osprey V](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Osprey_V "Osprey V"). The group's guitarist, Raji El-Jaru said that the group was influenced by Metallica and [Linkin Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linkin_Park "Linkin Park").[[304]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-305)
## Feuds
### Dave Mustaine and Megadeth
Former lead guitarist and [Megadeth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Megadeth "Megadeth") frontman [Dave Mustaine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dave_Mustaine "Dave Mustaine") has been involved in a historic feud with Metallica. During his time as a founding member during the first two years of the band, Mustaine's tenure was marred by his alcoholism and even a physical altercation with James Hetfield.[[305]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-306) Mustaine continued to make numerous inflammatory statements against his former band in the press due to buried tension primarily stemming from his firing in 1983 among other issues such as existing songwriting credits across Metallica's first three albums.[[306]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-307)[[307]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-308)
By the late 1980s, it was believed that the feud had largely subsided; later confirmed after Megadeth were hired to open for Metallica on numerous European dates in 1993.[[308]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-309) Despite this, Mustaine expressed his anger at Metallica during his appearance in the band's documentary _[Metallica: Some Kind of Monster](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica:_Some_Kind_of_Monster "Metallica: Some Kind of Monster")_ ,[[309]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-310) in a scene he later disapproved of as he felt he was mischaracterized, and that it did not represent the full extent of what happened during the meeting.[[310]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-311)
By 2010, the two bands found themselves on friendlier terms when they took part in the lauded "Big 4 Tour", alongside [Slayer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Slayer "Slayer") and [Anthrax](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anthrax_\(American_band\) "Anthrax \(American band\)").[[311]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-312) That same year, Mustaine took part in Metallica's exclusive 30th anniversary show in San Francisco which invited other former members: [Ron McGovney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ron_McGovney "Ron McGovney"), Lloyd Grant, and [Jason Newsted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jason_Newsted "Jason Newsted").[[312]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-313) In 2022, tensions reignited between the band and Mustaine following discussions over a remastered release of the _No Life 'Til Leather_ demo in which Hetfield and Ulrich considered involving Mustaine. However, the relationship quickly began to deteriorate once again after a dispute with Ulrich insisting on crediting himself on each of the tracks, much to Mustaine's anger, resulting in the cancellation of the project entirely and reigniting hostility with his former bandmates.[[313]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-314)[[314]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-315)[[315]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-316)
Often clashing during the early 1980s, both bands were fighting for dominance in the [Los Angeles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") rock scene during Metallica's early years prior to their relocation to the Bay Area. Both James Hetfield and Lars Ulrich expressed their hatred of the hair metal scene in Los Angeles during the time and frequently attacked multiple up-and-coming local bands in multiple interviews, including [Mötley Crüe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M%C3%B6tley_Cr%C3%BCe "Mötley Crüe").[[316]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-317) Ulrich and Mötley Crüe's bassist [Nikki Sixx](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nikki_Sixx "Nikki Sixx") had allegedly been involved in a spat prior to a show at the [Troubadour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Troubadour_\(West_Hollywood,_California\) "Troubadour \(West Hollywood, California\)") in [Hollywood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles") in 1982. Though despite their public disdain for glam metal, Metallica were impressed by the production and commercial success of _[Dr. Feelgood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dr._Feelgood_\(album\) "Dr. Feelgood \(album\)")_ in 1989, later hiring the album's producer [Bob Rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bob_Rock "Bob Rock") to produce their [self-titled fifth album](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica_\(album\) "Metallica \(album\)") in 1991.[[317]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-auto-318)[[318]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-auto2-319)
Several years later, Sixx and Ulrich reignited the feud after Ulrich accused Mötley Crüe of performing to a backing track at the [1997 American Music Awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1997_American_Music_Awards "1997 American Music Awards"). Sixx responded via an online forum in which he insulted numerous band members and also attacked Metallica's then-recent album _[Load](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Load_\(album\) "Load \(album\)")_.[[317]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-auto-318)[[318]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-auto2-319) Despite this, Sixx praised Metallica's then-latest album _[St. Anger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Anger "St. Anger")_ during a 2003 interview, contrary to the negative attention it received upon release.[[319]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-320)
In 2006, both Mötley Crüe drummer [Tommy Lee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tommy_Lee "Tommy Lee"), and former bassist Jason Newsted took part in a reality show titled _[Rock Star: Supernova](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock_Star:_Supernova "Rock Star: Supernova")_ as they would both participate in creating a supergroup while the show's premise revolved around finding a frontman via contest. During a 2011 meet-and-greet in [Mexico City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"), Hetfield was approached by a fan attacking Mötley Crüe due to their public image and frequent appearances in tabloids. Hetfield refused to directly respond to the fan's comments towards Mötley Crüe but laughed in his insults toward the band.[[320]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-321)
In 2015, drummer Lee reignited the feud when he tweeted a picture of Ulrich with the words "Straight Outta Tempo" superimposed over his face. Following the post by Lee, Mötley Crüe singer [Vince Neil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vince_Neil "Vince Neil") had stated online that he was unaware of the feud with Metallica; however, during a later interview with an [Oregon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oregon "Oregon") radio show, Neil was again asked about the band's current relationship with Metallica and hung up on the show's host.[[321]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-322) Things were alleged to have calmed down by 2017, when Hetfield appeared on Sixx's radio show _Sixx Sense_.[[322]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-323) During an interview in 2021, Sixx applauded Metallica's longevity and praised several of their releases during an interview.[[323]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-324)
### Jason Newsted
Following his hiring in 1986, former bassist [Jason Newsted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jason_Newsted "Jason Newsted") had regularly experienced incidents of [hazing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hazing "Hazing") and a perceived lack of acceptance from his bandmates despite his strong efforts in a majority of the band's live performances.[[324]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-325) Many fans and outlets cite the band's [fourth album](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/...And_Justice_for_All_\(album\) "...And Justice for All \(album\)")'s unusual mixing to drown the bass tracks as a possible effort by Hetfield and Ulrich to minimize Newsted's involvement.[[325]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-326)[[326]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-327) This sentiment was further exacerbated by the fact that Newsted was only given songwriting credits on three songs during his 14-year span in the band which also saw him perform on four studio albums.[[327]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-328)
Tensions between Hetfield and Newsted reached a breaking point in 2000 after Hetfield repeatedly blocked Newsted's request to venture into his side project [Echobrain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Echobrain "Echobrain").[[328]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-329)[[329]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-330) Discussions broke down and Newsted abruptly left the band in January 2001 prior to the recording of _[St. Anger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Anger "St. Anger")_.[[330]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-331) Despite Newsted's animosity surrounding his decision, he was later inducted into the hall of fame as a member of the band in 2009 and also appeared at the band's 30th anniversary show in San Francisco in 2011. In August 2021, to celebrate the 30th anniversary of the release of the band's self-titled album, Newsted was given a box set of the reissue to open for an [unboxing video](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unboxing_video "Unboxing video"), which was uploaded to the band's YouTube channel.[[331]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-332)[[332]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metallica#cite_note-333)

Storebrand Real Estate
# Your Nordic real estate partner
Storebrand Real Estate is the gateway and one-stop shop for investments in Nordic real estate supporting the green transition. We provide domestic and international investors access to building future-proof real estate portfolios.
## Storebrand Real Estate in figures
* * *
€8bn
* * *
AUM
70+
* * *
People
3
* * *
Countries
5/5
* * *
GRESB
![](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate/_/image/489a5553-e42a-4168-9e4f-150d3934efcb:df817443a53316b3216124d83197e29ca80b6865/block-1000-700/lconstruction-of-storebrand-headquarter-haakon-7-gate-oslo_1056x784px.jpg)
## Quality from the very beginning
Storebrand Real Estate is part of Storebrand Group, listed on the Oslo stock exchange, with a history dating back to 1767.
We are recognised globally as a leader in sustainable investment, and ranked by Dow Jones Sustainability Index to be among the top 10% most sustainable listed companies in the world.
[ About us ](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate/about-us)
![](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate/_/image/ff244e40-d4c8-42ce-aebf-2e63d85c876b:a769603be5883b69420af0faa4e27bafa021f587/block-1000-700/philip-pedersens-vei-einar-aslaksen-2_528x392.jpg)
## Future-proofing real estate
Storebrand is a pioneer in sustainable investments. We are proud to be part of a company being recognised as one of the world's 10 percent most sustainable listed companies, ranked by the Dow Jones Sustainability Index.  
  
All of our real estate portfolios have achieved five GRESB stars, the highest rating and recognition for being an industry leader.
[ How we work ](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate/sustainability)
![](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate/_/image/709f5083-aa16-416c-a42f-5ff9f755fa12:69e7804f985134b9ed0802d049436926b5d7ed90/block-1000-700/VIA-Vika-curve.jpg)
## Storebrand Sustainability Award
Storebrand Real Estate wants to be a driving force for the green shift in commercial real estate.
Storebrand Sustainability Award gives recognition to the real estate superstars - those who challenge established norms and share their knowledge to drive sustainability in the real estate industry.
[ About the award ](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate/sustainability/sustainability-award)
## Get more insight
  * [ Real Estate home page ](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate)
  * [ Portfolio and strategies ](https://www.storebrand.com/sam/no/asset-management/offerings/real-estate/portfolios-and-strategies)

Get in touch with our regional client executives  
to get advice and deep-dive into our products and services.
Get in touch

# Besan Ka Halwa
By [Manjula Jain](https://manjulaskitchen.com/home/about-manjula "Posts by Manjula Jain") • Published February 18, 2018
Jump to [Recipe ▼](https://manjulaskitchen.com/besan-ka-halwa/#recipe) [Ingredients ▼](https://manjulaskitchen.com/besan-ka-halwa/#recipe-12437-ingredients) [Instructions ▼](https://manjulaskitchen.com/besan-ka-halwa/#recipe-12437-instructions)
[23 Comments](https://manjulaskitchen.com/besan-ka-halwa/#comments)
17 minutes minutes
![Besan Ka Halwa](https://manjulaskitchen.com/wp-content/uploads/besan_ka_halwa-1024x576.jpg)
[![Besan Ka Halwa](https://manjulaskitchen.com/wp-content/uploads/besan_ka_halwa-500x500.jpg)](https://manjulaskitchen.com/wp-content/uploads/besan_ka_halwa.jpg)
## Besan Ka Halwa
Besan Ka Halwa is a traditional Indian [dessert](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/recipes/desserts/) and made using different flour, lentils, nuts and vegetables. Besan Ka Halwa is one of my favorite Halwas. This is delicious and also [quick and easy](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/miscellaneous/quick-easy/) to make. When you roast the besan, the house is full of sweet aroma. Besan Ka Halwa is also [gluten free](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/recipes/gluten-free/).
3.89 from 9 votes
[](https://manjulaskitchen.com/wprm_print/besan-ka-halwa) [](https://www.pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmanjulaskitchen.com%2Fbesan-ka-halwa%2F&media=https%3A%2F%2Fmanjulaskitchen.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2Fbesan_ka_halwa.jpg&description=Besan+Ka+Halwa&is_video=false)
Prep Time 5 minutes mins
Cook Time 12 minutes mins
Total Time 17 minutes mins
Course Dessert
Cuisine Indian
Servings [4](https://manjulaskitchen.com/besan-ka-halwa/) people
### Ingredients
1x2x3x
  * 1 cup coarse besan ladoo ka besan, gram flour
  * 1/2 cup clarified butter ghee
  * 1 cup sugar
  * 1/4 tsp cardamom powder ilachi
  * 2-1/2 cup water
  * 2 Tbsp almonds sliced


### Instructions
  * Boil the water with sugar, and cardamom powder in a sauce pan over low medium heat. Bring to a boil and Set aside.
  * Melt the clarified butter in a frying pan on low medium heat. Add besan and roast to golden brown color; stirring constantly this will take about 5 to 8 minutes. Besan will be golden brown in color and will have a light sweet aroma.
  * Add the syrup slowly, as you add the syrup to besan water will splatter.
  * Turn down the heat to low and let it cook for about 2-3 minutes.
  * Besan will absorb the water, as the Halwa cools so keep Halwa a little softer then you like.
  * Garnish with cashews.


### Notes
**Serving suggestion** Serve hot for best taste. If you like, garnish with shaved pistachio, almonds or shredded coconut.**Variations** Instead of water add 1 cup of milk and 1/2 cup of water and keep Halwa a little thicker. Pour the Halwa on a greased plate, keep it about 1/2 inch thick. Garnish with shaved almonds or pistachio or shredded coconut, and let it cool for about a half an hour. Cut the Halwa in square or diamond shapes like burfi.
Tried this recipe?[Let us know](https://manjulaskitchen.com/besan-ka-halwa/#comment) how it was!
Besan ka Halwa is a classic Indian [dessert](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/recipes/desserts/) made from besan (gram flour), ghee (clarified butter), sugar, and aromatic spices. This sweet treat is loved for its rich flavor, creamy texture, and irresistible aroma. 
Whether served warm or at room temperature, Besan ka Halwa is a delightful dessert that’s perfect for celebrating special occasions such as a [party recipe](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/special-occasions/party-recipes/) or even festive occasions such as [diwali](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/holidays-festivals/diwali-holidays-festivals/), holi, ganesh chaturthi or [janmashtami](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/holidays-festivals/krishna-janmashtami/) or simply made to satisfy one’s sweet tooth. 
### Variations of Halwa:
Although Besan ka Halwa can be considered to be one of the most savoury and fulfilling desserts amongst all halwas, here we have a list of some halwas that can be savoured and enjoyed all along with friends and family. 
  1. [Gajar ka Halwa](https://manjulaskitchen.com/gajar-ka-halwa-carrot-halwa/): Gajar Ka Halwa is a classical Indian dessert, this is also known as gajraula. Gajar ka Halwa is a delicious and popular sweet made with few ingredients, carrots, milk, sugar and flavoured with cardamom.
  2. [Atta Ka Halwa](https://manjulaskitchen.com/atta-ka-halwa/): Atta ka Halwa, is a delicious sweet dessert. This is easy to make and with few ingredients. Atta ka halwa is made with whole wheat flour, sugar, and clarified butter, flavoured with cardamom.
  3. [Sooji ka Halwa](https://manjulaskitchen.com/sooji-halwa/): Sooji Halwa is a very popular sweet dish all over India and is made on religious occasions, as prasad.
  4. [Sweet Potato Halwa](https://manjulaskitchen.com/sweet-potato-halwa-eggless-pudding/): Sweet Potato Halwa, an eggless pudding, stands out as one of the simplest Halwa recipes to prepare. Infused with the delicate and distinctive flavors of cardamom and cinnamon, it serves as a deliciously warm dessert ideal for chilly winter evenings.


### Tips for Making Perfect Besan ka Halwa:
**Use Good Quality Gram Flour:** Use fresh and high-quality gram flour (besan) to make Besan ka Halwa for the best flavor and texture. Fresh besan has a nutty aroma and a smooth texture, which enhances the taste of the halwa.
**Roast the Gram Flour Well:** Roast the gram flour in ghee until it turns golden brown and fragrant. Properly roasting the gram flour is crucial for developing the rich and nutty flavor of the halwa.
**Control the Sweetness:** Adjust the amount of sugar according to your taste preferences. Some people prefer Besan ka Halwa to be sweeter, while others prefer it less sweet. Start with a smaller amount of sugar and add more if needed.
**Garnish with Nuts and Saffron:** Garnish Besan ka Halwa with chopped nuts such as almonds, pistachios, and cashews, as well as a few strands of saffron for a luxurious touch. The nuts add crunch and texture, while saffron adds color and aroma to the halwa.
### FAQs about Besan ka Halwa:
#### Can I make Besan ka Halwa ahead of time? 
Yes, Besan ka Halwa can be made ahead of time and stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat the halwa gently on the stovetop or in the microwave before serving.
#### Can I freeze Besan ka Halwa? 
While Besan ka Halwa can be frozen, its texture may change slightly upon thawing. If freezing, allow the halwa to cool completely before transferring it to a freezer-safe container. Thaw the halwa in the refrigerator overnight before reheating.
#### Is Besan ka Halwa vegan-friendly? 
Traditional Besan ka Halwa is made with ghee (clarified butter), which is not vegan. However, you can make a vegan version of Besan ka Halwa by using plant-based ghee or oil instead of dairy ghee.
#### Is Besan ka Halwa gluten-free? 
Yes, Besan ka Halwa is gluten-free as it is made from gram flour, which is naturally [gluten-free](https://manjulaskitchen.com/category/special-diets/gluten-free/). However, be cautious of cross-contamination if you have a severe gluten allergy or celiac disease.
Besan ka Halwa is a luxurious and aromatic dessert that’s perfect for indulging in on special occasions or as a sweet treat for your loved ones. 
With its rich flavor, creamy texture, and endless variations, Besan ka Halwa is sure to become a favorite in your dessert repertoire. Whether enjoyed warm or at room temperature, this halwa is guaranteed to satisfy your sweet cravings and leave you feeling content and happy.
Besan ka Halwa goes best even at marriage and big events hence its often served as people’s favorite choice of sweet dessert after every celebration and is a great hit among family gatherings and unions.

metadata.author: Manjula Jain
metadata.description: Besan Halwa - Treat yourself to a taste of heaven with our Besan Halwa recipe. Indulge in the rich flavors and aromatic essence of this traditional Indian sweet dish.
metadata.og:description: Besan Ka Halwa, Halwa is a traditional Indian dessert and made using different flour, lentils, nuts and vegetables. Besan Ka Halwa is one of my favorite Halwa. This is a delicious also quick and easy to make. When you roast the besan, house is full of sweet aroma. Besan Ka Halwa is also gluten free.
metadata.og:title: Besan Ka Halwa

**Halva** (also **halvah** , **halwa** , **halua**[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-NYT.Nos-3)) is a type of [confectionery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Confectionery "Confectionery") that is spread throughout [North Africa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_Africa "North Africa"), [East Africa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Africa "East Africa"), [Eastern Europe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eastern_Europe "Eastern Europe"), and the [Balkans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Balkans "Balkans"), [West Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Asia "West Asia"), [Central Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Asia "Central Asia"), and [South Asia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Asia "South Asia"). The name refers to a broad variety of recipes, generally a thick paste made from flour, butter, oil, saffron, [rosewater](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosewater "Rosewater"), milk, [turmeric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric "Turmeric") powder, and sugar.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Davidson-4)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-5)[[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-6)[[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-7)
## Etymology
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]
[![Wiktionary logo](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg/40px-Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg)
Look up _**[halva](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/Special:Search/halva "wiktionary:Special:Search/halva")**_ in Wiktionary, the free dictionary.
The word _halva_ entered the English language between 1840 and 1850 from Romanian, which came from [Ottoman Turkish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ottoman_Turkish_language "Ottoman Turkish language"): حلوى, [romanized](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romanization_of_Ottoman_Turkish "Romanization of Ottoman Turkish"):  _helva_ , itself ultimately derived from [Arabic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): حلاوة, [romanized](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romanization_of_Arabic "Romanization of Arabic"):  _ḥalwā_ , a sweet confection.[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Marks_Encyclopedia-8)[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-D-9)[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-:1-10) The root in Arabic: ح ل و, romanized:  __ḥ-l-w__ , means "sweet".[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-11) In the pre-Islamic period, the name for the category of confections that would develop into _halva_ were called روغن خورديگ (_rōɣæn xordīg_) in [Middle Persian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Middle_Persian "Middle Persian"), meaning "oil food".[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-:0-1)
## History
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]
Halva originated in [Persia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Name_of_Iran "Name of Iran") (modern day [Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran "Iran")).[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-:0-1)[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-12) By the 9th century, the term was applied to numerous kinds of sweets, including the now-familiar sweetened cooked semolina or flour paste.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Davidson-4)[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Marks_Encyclopedia-8) The first proper recipes of halva were documented in the 13th century [Arabic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arabic "Arabic") cookbook _Kitab al-Tabikh_ (_The Book of Dishes_), as well as an anonymous cookbook from 13th-century [Al-Andalus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al-Andalus "Al-Andalus"). 
[Athenaeus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Athenaeus "Athenaeus") mentions an [ancient Greek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Greece "Ancient Greece") dish called "_sesamidios_ " ([Ancient Greek](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Greek "Ancient Greek"): σησαμίδιός), which is described as a spherical cake made of honey, roasted sesame seeds, and oil; this dish is considered to have been the same as the sesame seed-based version of halva.[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-:4-13) Halva was also eaten in the [Byzantine Empire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Byzantine_Empire "Byzantine Empire"), where it was well-attested.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-14) Later, it was adopted by the [Ottoman Turks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ottoman_Turks "Ottoman Turks"), including the sesame-based version, and spread throughout their empire.[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Marks_Encyclopedia-8) A description of sesame-based halwa (called [Arabic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): حلاوة طحينية) can be found in an 1844 dictionary by Swedish Orientalist [Jacob Berggren](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Jacob_Berggren&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jacob Berggren \(page does not exist\)") [[sv](https://sv.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jacob_Berggren "sv:Jacob Berggren")],[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-berggren-15) according to [Al Adib](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Al_Adib "Al Adib") magazine, a factory for tahini halwa was established as early as 1874 in [Beirut](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beirut "Beirut").[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-16)
## Types
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Types")]
Most types of halva are relatively dense confections sweetened with sugar or [honey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Honey "Honey").[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-17) Their textures, however, vary. For example, [semolina](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semolina "Semolina")-based halva's texture can range from a buttery, moist, clumpy couscous[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-18) to something gelatinous and translucent, while sesame-based halva is drier and more crumbly.[[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-19)
### Grain-based halva
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Grain-based halva")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2e/Un_helva.jpg/250px-Un_helva.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Un_helva.jpg)Turkish _un helvası_ , a flour-based halva
Grain-based halva is made by toasting flour or cornstarch in oil, mixing it into a [roux](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roux "Roux"), and then cooking it with a sugary syrup. Corn is rarely used. 
Dishes made from wheat [semolina](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semolina "Semolina") include [suji ka halwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suji_ka_halwa "Suji ka halwa") in India, Pakistan, Shujir Halua in Bangladesh and __irmik helvası__ in Turkey. In both dishes, semolina is toasted in fat, either oil or butter, and then mixed with water or milk and sugar to achieve the desired flavor and consistency.[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-segnit-20) Wheat-based [sohan halwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sohan_halwa "Sohan halwa") in northern India and Pakistan is a renowned delicacy made by combining wheat flour with milk, sugar, [clarified butter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clarified_butter "Clarified butter"), cardamom, [saffron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saffron "Saffron"), and nuts such as almonds and pistachios. The mixture is slow-cooked, allowing the sugar to [caramelize](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caramelization "Caramelization"), which gives _Sōhan_ _halvā_ its unique firm and brittle texture. 
[![Multani sohan halva](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Multani-sohan-halwa.jpg/250px-Multani-sohan-halwa.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Multani-sohan-halwa.jpg)Multani Sōhan halvā in Pakistan.
Dairy-based rice flour halva, known as [Pathein halawa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pathein_halawa "Pathein halawa"), is considered a Burmese delicacy native to the city of [Pathein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pathein "Pathein"). 
### Sesame
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sesame")]
[Sesame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sesame "Sesame") halva is popular in the Balkans, [Poland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poland "Poland"), the Middle East, and other areas surrounding the Mediterranean Sea. 
The primary ingredients in this confection are sesame butter or paste ([tahini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tahini "Tahini")), and sugar, glucose or honey.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Davidson-4) [Soapwort](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Common_Soapwort "Common Soapwort")[[21]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-21)[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-22) (called _‘erq al halaweh_ in Arabic; __çöven__ in Turkish), or egg white are added in some recipes to stabilize the oils in the mixture or create a distinctive texture for the resulting confection. Other ingredients and flavorings, such as [pistachio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pistachio "Pistachio") nuts, [cocoa powder](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cocoa_powder "Cocoa powder"), [orange juice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orange_juice "Orange juice"), [vanilla](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanilla "Vanilla"), or chocolate are often added to the basic tahini and sugar base.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Davidson-4)[[23]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-23)
### Sunflower
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Sunflower")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/93/Sonnenblumenhalva_20091117_005.JPG/250px-Sonnenblumenhalva_20091117_005.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Sonnenblumenhalva_20091117_005.JPG)[Sunflower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sunflower "Sunflower") halva
[Sunflower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sunflower "Sunflower") halva is popular in the [countries of the former Soviet Union](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Post-Soviet_states "Post-Soviet states") as well as in [Bulgaria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria") and [Romania](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romania "Romania").[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-24) It is made of roasted ground [sunflower seeds](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sunflower_seed "Sunflower seed") instead of sesame. It may include other ingredients, such as nuts, cocoa powder, or vanilla.[[25]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-25)[[26]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-gosstandart-26) In 1996 around 4–5 thousand tonnes of sunflower halva were being produced by Ukraine annually.[[27]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-27)

### Peanuts
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Peanuts")]
In Argentina, Greek immigrants at the beginning of the 20th century created a kind of halva called _[mantecol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mantecol "Mantecol")_ from peanut butter, currently marketed under the name of Mantecol and also Nucrem. Such a product is widely consumed in the country. 
### Carrots
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Carrots")]
[Gajar ka halwa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gajar_ka_halwa "Gajar ka halwa"), or gajorer halua, is a popular halva in India, Pakistan and Bangladesh. It is made by slow-cooking grated carrots with milk, sugar, and ghee (clarified butter), often flavored with cardamom and garnished with nuts such as almonds, pistachios, or cashews. Sometimes, khoya (reduced milk solids) or condensed milk is added to enhance its richness and flavor. 
### Other
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Other")]
#### Floss halva
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Floss halva")]
See also: [Pashmak](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pashmak "Pashmak"), [Pişmaniye](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pi%C5%9Fmaniye "Pişmaniye"), and [Dragon's beard candy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dragon%27s_beard_candy "Dragon's beard candy")
_[Pişmaniye](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pi%C5%9Fmaniye "Pişmaniye")_ ([Turkish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkish_language "Turkish language")) or _floss halva_ is a traditional sweet, prepared in [Kocaeli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kocaeli_Province "Kocaeli Province"), Turkey, made by flossing thin strands of halva into a light confection. Made primarily of wheat flour and sugar, the strands are continuously wrapped into a ball shape and then compressed. The result is a halva with a light consistency, similar to [cotton candy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cotton_candy "Cotton candy"). Floss halva can be found in regular and pistachio flavors, and there are brands with [halal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halal "Halal") or [kosher](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kosher "Kosher") certifications. 
In Chinese cuisine, a floss-like candy similar to _pişmaniye_ or _pashmak halva_ , known as _[dragon beard candy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dragon_beard_candy "Dragon beard candy")_ , is eaten as a snack or dessert. 
A raw version of halva also has become popular among proponents of raw food diets. In this version, a mixture of raw sesame [tahini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tahini "Tahini"), raw [almonds](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Almonds "Almonds"), raw [agave nectar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agave_nectar "Agave nectar") and salt are blended together and frozen to firm.[[28]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-28)
## Cultural and national variations
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Cultural and national variations")]
### Albania
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Albania")]
Albanian halva ([Albanian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albanian_language "Albanian language"): _hallvë_) tends to have flour, sugar and olive oil and is similar to nougat. It is often topped with nuts.[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-29)[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-30)
### Armenia
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Armenia")]
Armenian halva tends to have semolina flour, sesame, honey, butter and sugar.[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-31)
### Azerbaijan
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Azerbaijan")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/33/Halva_Small.JPG/250px-Halva_Small.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Halva_Small.JPG)Şəki halvası
One regional variant is from [Sheki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shaki,_Azerbaijan "Shaki, Azerbaijan") where _[Şəki halvası](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sheki_Halva "Sheki Halva")_ halva refers to a layered [bakhlava](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bakhlava "Bakhlava") style pastry filled with spiced-nut mix and topped by crisscrossed patterns of a red syrup made from saffron, dried carrot and beetroot.[[32]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-32)[[33]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-33)
### Greece
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Greece")]
Halva is a traditional fasting food among Greeks who traditionally have food restrictions due to Greek Orthodox fasting periods, especially from meat, on Wednesdays and Fridays throughout the year, and for all of [Great Lent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Lent "Great Lent").[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-34) Halva is also frequently served as a desert, or at "paniyiria" (village festivals). 
It is most commonly made of sesame or semolina throughout Greece, however, one regional variation originates from the Thessaly town of [Farsala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Farsala "Farsala") (known as "Halva Farsalon") where it is made of cornflour.[[35]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-35)
### Italy
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Italy")]
Halva has become popular in Italy. In 2023, the artisanal confectionery laboratory The Halva Lab was established in Sassuolo, producing halva inspired by Mediterranean and Middle Eastern traditions but made without added sugars.[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-36)[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-37)
### India
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: India")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cb/Halva1.jpg/250px-Halva1.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Halva1.jpg)Some assorted Indian halva including _sooji halva_ (diamond shapes), _chana halva_ (light circles), and _gajar halva_ (dark circles)
India has many types of halva, some unique to particular regions of the country. It is one of the popular sweets of India usually made from semolina.[[38]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-38)
The town of [Bhatkal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhatkal "Bhatkal") in [Coastal Karnataka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karavali "Karavali") is famous for its unique banana halwa which is infused with either whole cashews, pistachio or almonds. This type of authentic halwa is a specialty of the Muslims of this town. The Udupi cuisine has halwa made from banana, wheat, ashgourd, and jackfruit.[[39]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-39)[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-40)
It is speculated that halva (or halwa) is associated with Indian traditions and culture. Written records of sweets from [Mānasollāsa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manasollasa "Manasollasa") mention a sweet called _shali-anna_ , a type of semolina halwa which is today known as [kesari](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kesari_bat "Kesari bat") in South India.[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-41)
[Tirunelveli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tirunelveli_district "Tirunelveli district") in Tamil Nadu is known for its wheat halwa. Its preparation is a laborious process that "is slowly seeing this sweet disappear." Unlike other sweets, the extra ghee is not drained out but forms an outer layer. This increases the shelf life of the halwa. Locals attribute the unique taste of the halwa to the water of the [Thamirabarani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thamirabarani_River "Thamirabarani River").[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-42)
The history of [Kozhikode](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kozhikode "Kozhikode") Halwa in [Kerala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kerala "Kerala") could trace back to [Zamorin era](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamorin_of_Calicut "Zamorin of Calicut"). Zamorin invited chefs from [Gujarat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gujarat "Gujarat") to prepare halwa for their royal feast.[[43]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-43) They were also granted places to stay beside royal kitchen. This settlement later evolved as sweet sellers street, nowadays known as [SM (Sweet Meat) Street](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S._M._Street "S. M. Street") or [Mittayitheruvu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/S._M._Street "S. M. Street").[[44]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-44) Kozhikode halwa is made of pure coconut oil, not from ghee. Kozhikode halwa also builds religious harmony; [Ayyappa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ayyappan "Ayyappan") devotees from neighboring states [Karnataka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karnataka "Karnataka") and [Andhra Pradesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andhra_Pradesh "Andhra Pradesh") buy halwa and chips like prasadam (sacred food). They distribute them among their neighbors and friends, who consume them with a religious zeal.[[45]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-45)
  * [![Sooji halwa made from semolina or sooji](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c0/Sooji_Halwa_%28Semolina_Pudding%29.JPG/120px-Sooji_Halwa_%28Semolina_Pudding%29.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Sooji_Halwa_\(Semolina_Pudding\).JPG "Sooji halwa made from semolina or sooji")
Sooji halwa made from semolina or sooji
  * [![Black halwa from Kerala](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/Black_Halwa%2C_Kerala.jpg/120px-Black_Halwa%2C_Kerala.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Black_Halwa,_Kerala.jpg "Black halwa from Kerala")
Black halwa from Kerala


### Iran
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Iran")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Wedding.Celebration.Halva.webp/250px-Wedding.Celebration.Halva.webp.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Wedding.Celebration.Halva.webp)Platters of halva are served with a cake, a jelly pudding, and cooked chickens in Iranian wedding celebrations
In [Iran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran "Iran"), _halva_ ([Persian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Persian_language "Persian language"): حلوا) usually refers to a related confection made from wheat flour and butter and flavored with [saffron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saffron "Saffron") and [rose water](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rose_water "Rose water").[[46]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-46)[[47]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-47) The final product has a yellow, brown, or dark brown color. The halva is spread thin on a plate and left until it dries into a paste. Halva usually is served at wedding celebrations, religious ceremonies and funerals. 
_Halva ardeh_ is the Iranian term for tahini-based halva, and may or may not include whole pistachios. _Ardeh_ is processed sesame in the form of paste, usually sweetened with [syrup](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syrup "Syrup").[[48]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-48)[[49]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-49)
### Israel
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Israel")]
Tahini halvah ([Hebrew](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hebrew_language "Hebrew language"): חלווה) is very popular in Israel and among Jews in the diaspora.[[50]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-50)[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-51) Israeli halvah is made from [sesame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sesame "Sesame") [tahini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tahini "Tahini") and sugar. It is generally sold in slabs, with or without nuts. Vanilla, or vanilla with chocolate swirls are perhaps the most common, but there are many different varieties. Halvah is [parve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parve "Parve"). It is often served as a breakfast component at Israeli hotels. 
It is also used in specialty ice cream, which is made of sesame halva, [tahini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tahini "Tahini"), eggs, cream, and sugar, and usually topped with [pistachios](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pistachios "Pistachios") and [Silan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rub_\(syrup\) "Rub \(syrup\)") (date syrup).[[52]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-52)[[53]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-53)
  * [![Israeli halva displays at the Mahane Yehuda Market in Jerusalem](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e1/Jerusalem_DSC_0764_%288936121697%29.jpg/120px-Jerusalem_DSC_0764_%288936121697%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Jerusalem_DSC_0764_\(8936121697\).jpg "Israeli halva displays at the Mahane Yehuda Market in Jerusalem")
Israeli halva displays at the [Mahane Yehuda Market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahane_Yehuda_Market "Mahane Yehuda Market") in [Jerusalem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jerusalem "Jerusalem")
  * [![Halva ice cream](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/05/Halva_Icecream_P1130769.JPG/120px-Halva_Icecream_P1130769.JPG)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Halva_Icecream_P1130769.JPG "Halva ice cream")
Halva ice cream


[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Myanmar \(Burma\)")]
In [Myanmar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") (Burma), _halawa_ (ဟလဝါ) generally refers to [Pathein halawa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pathein_halawa "Pathein halawa") (ပုသိမ်ဟလဝါ), a Burmese confection or _[mont](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mont_\(food\) "Mont \(food\)")_ made with [glutinous rice flour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glutinous_rice_flour "Glutinous rice flour"), [rice flour](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rice_flour "Rice flour"), milk, and coconut shavings originating in the [Irrawaddy delta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Irrawaddy_delta "Irrawaddy delta") town of [Pathein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pathein "Pathein"). Another popular [semolina](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semolina "Semolina")-based confection, which is known as sooji halawa in [India](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India "India"), is called [sanwin makin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sanwin_makin "Sanwin makin") in Myanmar. 
### Palestine
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Palestine")]
In the [West Bank](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/West_Bank "West Bank"), [qizha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qizha "Qizha") (nigella sativa paste) flavored tahini halwa is traditionally made,[[54]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-54) as well as an "angel-hair" [candy-floss](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cotton_candy "Cotton candy")-like variety of tahini halwa.[[55]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-55)[[56]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-56)
### Syria
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Syria")]
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/%D8%AD%D9%84%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%A9_%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%85%D8%AD%D9%8A%D8%A7_-_%D9%85%D8%AD%D8%B4%D9%8A%D8%A9.jpg/250px-%D8%AD%D9%84%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%A9_%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%85%D8%AD%D9%8A%D8%A7_-_%D9%85%D8%AD%D8%B4%D9%8A%D8%A9.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:%D8%AD%D9%84%D8%A7%D9%88%D8%A9_%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%85%D8%AD%D9%8A%D8%A7_-_%D9%85%D8%AD%D8%B4%D9%8A%D8%A9.jpg)Halawet al-mahya, Syria
In [Aleppo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aleppo "Aleppo"), [Syria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syria "Syria"), _Ma'mounia_ (مأمونية), named after [Caliph Al-Ma'mun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caliph_Al-Ma%27mun "Caliph Al-Ma'mun"), is a variety of halwa made by cooking semolina in butter and then drenching it in syrup, creating something similar to [semolina pudding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Semolina_pudding "Semolina pudding"), modern varieties add hard [white cheese](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/White_cheese "White cheese") to the dessert.[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Davidson-4)[[57]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-57) Syrian historian [Khayr al-Din al-Asadi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khayr_al-Din_al-Asadi "Khayr al-Din al-Asadi") described 22 varieties of halwa being made in [Aleppo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aleppo "Aleppo") in his 1971 encyclopedia.[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-aleppoEnc1981-58) _Halawat smeed_ (حلاوة سميد) is sometimes used to refer to varieties of semolina pudding.[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-59)[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-60)[[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Davidson-4)
_Halawet al-mahya_ ([Arabic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): حلاوة المحيا) is a semolina and [qatir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Qatir_\(syrup\) "Qatir \(syrup\)") based halwa made during the month of [Sha'ban](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sha%27ban "Sha'ban") in the city of [Hama](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hama "Hama"), Syria.[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-61)[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-aleppoEnc1981-58)
### Turkey
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: Turkey")]
In [Turkey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkey "Turkey") halva is served for special occasions such as births, circumcisions, weddings and religious gatherings. The tradition is for semolina halva to be served at funerals, when someone leaves or returns from [Hajj](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hajj "Hajj"), and during [Ramadan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sawm "Sawm").[[62]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-deathFish-62)
For this reason, flour (_un_) halva is also called in [Turkish](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turkish_language "Turkish language") _ölü helvası_ , meaning "halva of the dead". The expression "roasting halva for someone" suggests that the person referred to has died. 
### United States
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: United States")]
Halva can be found in ethnic Indian, Jewish,[[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-Marks_Encyclopedia-8) Arab, Persian, Greek, Balkan community stores and delicatessens as well as natural food stores. Besides being imported, it is manufactured in the United States, with the largest producer being Brooklyn-originated [Joyva](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joyva "Joyva").[[63]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-NYT.BK-63)[[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-NYT.Nos-3)
### Somalia
[[edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Halva&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Somalia")]
In [Somalia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Somalia "Somalia"), halva is known as xalwo (also spelled halwo or xalwa). It is a popular sweet made from sugar, oil, and cornstarch, flavored with spices such as cardamom, nutmeg, or cloves, and sometimes enriched with peanuts. Xalwo is traditionally served at weddings, Eid celebrations, and other festive occasions, and is often offered to guests alongside Somali tea or coffee as a symbol of hospitality.[[64]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halva#cite_note-64)

# Velkommen til din nye drømmehytte på Sørlandet!
## Nyoppført og meget tiltalene hytte med fantastisk utsikt over trysfjorden
Her kan man sikre seg en meget lekker ny og innflyttningsklar hytte på Skarpeid i Søgne. Umiddelbar nærhet til Ålo og Trysnes samt indrefileen av hva Søgne skjærgården kan tilby. Hytten har nydlig utsikt til Trysfjorden fra stue/kjøkken, 1 soverom, terrassen og takterrassen. Meget gode solforhold. Innhold: Gang, bod med egen utgang. Stue med spiseplass og utgang terrasse, åpen kjøkkenløsning med delikat innredning og integrerte hvitevarer. 2 bad med dusjhjørne, baderomsinnredning og vegghengt wc hvorav det ene har direkte inngang fra hovedsoveromet. 3 soverom og gang/TV-stue. Utrolig flott og stor flislagt takterrasse med opplegg for utekjøkken. Mulighet for kjøp eller leie av båtplass i ny båthavn som er under bygging. Denne hytten og beliggenheten må bare oppleves, velkommen til visning!
## Ta kontakt med megler :
Geir Flaa Johansen - Eiendomsmegler MNEF Telefon: [457 70 000](tel:457%2070%20000) Mobil: [950 00 000](tel:950%2000%20000)
[](https://www.finn.no/realestate/leisuresale/ad.html?finnkode=144765617 "Se annonse på FINN.NO")
Beliggenhet: Ved sjøen Boligtype: Hytte Soverom: 3 Primærrom: 90 m² Bruksareal: 98 m² Tomteareal: 405 m² (eiet) Eieform: Eier (Selveier) Byggeår: 2018 Rom: 5 Sengeplasser: 8 
### Fasiliteter
  * Balkong/Terrasse
  * Fiskemulighet
  * Innlagt vann
  * Innlagt strøm
  * Turterreng
  * Offentlig vann/kloakk
  * Utsikt
  * Bilvei frem
  * Båtplass


###### Bølgen Eiendomsutvikling AS
Frode Haaland: [950 73 101](tlf:950%2073%20101)  
Jon Kleiveland: [482 40 910](tlf:482%2040%20910)
### Andre Prosjekter:
## PROSJEKTERT HYTTE MED ALT PÅ EN FLATE OG UTSIKT TIL TRYSFJORDEN
Hytten får følgende innhold: Gang, bod/ vaskerom, bad, 4 soverom, stue og kjøkken. Mulighet for å påvirke materialvalg og innhold hvis man kommer tidlig inn i byggeprosessen. Blant annet er det mulighet for takterrasse og 2 bad.
[](https://www.finn.no/realestate/leisuresale/ad.html?finnkode=163902716 "Se annonse på FINN.NO")
## NY HYTTE I SØGNES SKJÆRGÅRD MED FLOTT TAKTERRASSE
Hytten er delvis ferdig utvendig men ikke innredet. Her er det muligheter for å påvirke innredning og interiør etter dine ønsker. 
[](https://www.finn.no/realestate/leisuresale/ad.html?finnkode=151771787 "Se annonse på FINN.NO")

## Vi feirer 80 år!
Norske skolesekker, ergonomisk formet og elsket siden 1946.
## Anmeldelser
> ★★★★★
> **Flott ryggsekk**
> Rask levering, veldig kul sekk, praktisk og lett, barnet mitt er veldig fornøyd.
> Barbara
> Lübeck, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> **Skolesekk**
> Virkelig en flott skolesekk! Topp kvalitet, veldig lett og flott utseende! rask levering!
> Tamara
> Frohnleiten, Østerrike
> ★★★★★
> **Flott sekk**
> Sitter veldig fint på ryggen. Praktisk med eget rom for vannflaske og matboks. Åpningen gjør det enkelt å sette ting inn og ta dem ut. Flott at det kan utvides. Datteren min elsker det.
> Vigdis
> Drammen, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Flott skolesekk**
> God kvalitet og virkelig gjennomtenkt. En god mellomløsning mellom den stive bokslignende skolesekken og en høykvalitets ryggsekk. Veldig behagelig og lett. Design er også flott for kule barn.
> Maren
> Heilbronn, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Alt klart til skolestart
> Superglad førskolegutt har nesten sovet med sekken om natten. Praktisk veske med skrå glidelås og alt tilbehøret du trenger. Anbefalt.
> Erik
> Risør, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Helt fornøyd**
> Som vanlig er servicen på topp, rask levering og den nye vesken er virkelig deilig og passer SÅ godt på en høy slank jente.
> Britta
> Nexø, Danmark
> ★★★★★
> Jeg er begeistret for Beckmann skolesekkene og tilbehøret som matboksene/drikkeflaskene. Den klassiske skolesekken er perfekt for små barn, på 107 cm. Jeg vil gjerne anbefale det :)
> Daniela
> Bielefeld, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Veldig fin sekk. Den har alt du trenger til skolehverdagen. Jeg vil anbefale denne sekken fra Beckmann. Hilsen Lydia 10 år
> Hilsen Lydia 10 år
> Oslo, Norge

## Vi bryr oss om vår verden
Bak hver sekk og hvert produkt vi utvikler, ligger en rekke bevisste valg.  
Vi jobber for at hele verdikjeden vår skal være preget av respekt, rettferdighet og omtanke.

## Vi feirer 80 år!
Norske skolesekker, ergonomisk formet og elsket siden 1946.
## Anmeldelser
> ★★★★★
> **Flott ryggsekk**
> Rask levering, veldig kul sekk, praktisk og lett, barnet mitt er veldig fornøyd.
> Barbara
> Lübeck, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> **Skolesekk**
> Virkelig en flott skolesekk! Topp kvalitet, veldig lett og flott utseende! rask levering!
> Tamara
> Frohnleiten, Østerrike
> ★★★★★
> **Flott sekk**
> Sitter veldig fint på ryggen. Praktisk med eget rom for vannflaske og matboks. Åpningen gjør det enkelt å sette ting inn og ta dem ut. Flott at det kan utvides. Datteren min elsker det.
> Vigdis
> Drammen, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Flott skolesekk**
> God kvalitet og virkelig gjennomtenkt. En god mellomløsning mellom den stive bokslignende skolesekken og en høykvalitets ryggsekk. Veldig behagelig og lett. Design er også flott for kule barn.
> Maren
> Heilbronn, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Alt klart til skolestart
> Superglad førskolegutt har nesten sovet med sekken om natten. Praktisk veske med skrå glidelås og alt tilbehøret du trenger. Anbefalt.
> Erik
> Risør, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Helt fornøyd**
> Som vanlig er servicen på topp, rask levering og den nye vesken er virkelig deilig og passer SÅ godt på en høy slank jente.
> Britta
> Nexø, Danmark
> ★★★★★
> Jeg er begeistret for Beckmann skolesekkene og tilbehøret som matboksene/drikkeflaskene. Den klassiske skolesekken er perfekt for små barn, på 107 cm. Jeg vil gjerne anbefale det :)
> Daniela
> Bielefeld, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Veldig fin sekk. Den har alt du trenger til skolehverdagen. Jeg vil anbefale denne sekken fra Beckmann. Hilsen Lydia 10 år

## Vi bryr oss om vår verden
Bak hver sekk og hvert produkt vi utvikler, ligger en rekke bevisste valg.  
Vi jobber for at hele verdikjeden vår skal være preget av respekt, rettferdighet og omtanke.

# Funding for five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk vessels
20/06/2024
[ ![Funding awarded to realise five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk newbuilds](https://workboat365.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Group-photo_Maris-Fiducia-HAV-Hydrogen-Norwegian-Hydrogen.webp) ](https://workboat365.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Group-photo_Maris-Fiducia-HAV-Hydrogen-Norwegian-Hydrogen.webp)Foto from left: From the left: Quint Vedder , Maris Fiducia; Jørgen Kopperstad, Norwegian Hydrogen; Richard Inkster, Maris Fiducia Norway; Kristian Osnes, HAV Hydrogen.
**Norwegian state-owned Enova has awarded NOK 300 million in innovation funding to Maris Fiducia Norway AS, with HAV Hydrogen and Norwegian Hydrogen as subcontractors, to develop, build and operate five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk vessels.**
The NOK 300 million grant is awarded under [Enova](https://www.enova.no/)’s “hydrogen in vessels”-programme, which aims to contribute to market change by supporting companies that will use hydrogen as fuel in vessels and support the development of zero emission vessel technologies.
Maris Fiducia Norway, which is the Norwegian operation of Dutch shipowner Maris Fiducia, will manage and operate the five vessels. When built the five dry-bulk carriers will go on hire through a zero-emission time charter agreement with Schulte & Bruns, establishing a commercial operation for vessels operating on hydrogen fuel, proving the feasibility of hydrogen technologies for shipping.
The 6,000 dwt vessels are designed by the Dutch ship designer Ankerbeer with a hull design optimised for efficiency and low fuel consumption. The power plant shall consist of a dual fuel hydrogen ICE combined with fuel cells for auxiliary power.
“We are thrilled that Enova recognised the strong case for the project with this award which is an important milestone for our team in Norway. A little over one year ago we were inspired by a conversation with Jørgen Kopperstad from Norwegian Hydrogen about fuel availability and technical competence in the Norwegian maritime cluster. It has been quite a journey since then, but we are only just starting,” says Markku Vedder, CEO of the Maris Fiducia Group.
In addition to the Dual Fuel ICE, the plan is to equip the five vessels with a Zero Emission Pod – ZEPOD® – a module containing a complete hydrogen energy system for ships, delivered by Norway-based HAV Hydrogen.
Each of the ZEPODs are intended to be delivered with a total capacity of 400 kW, which is enough to run all auxiliary systems on board the bulk carriers. The ZEPODs can be upgraded to 1 MW capacity at a later stage.
HAV Hydrogen is currently developing its first full-scale [ZEPOD,](https://workboat365.com/dnv-has-awarded-hav-group-asa-preliminary-approval-for-its-hydrogen-based-energy-system-2/) which will be ready for testing next year. Preliminary delivery schedule shows that the sequence of delivery will commence from the end of 2025, depending on shipyard.
“This brings us another step closer to the commercial breakthrough for our hydrogen-based energy systems for ships. Everyone knows that the shipping industry needs to lower emissions, but it relies on companies such as Maris Fiducia Norway to pave the way. We are delighted to be part of this partnership and to help develop the future for short sea cargo together with Maris Fiducia and Norwegian Hydrogen,” says Kristian Osnes, managing director of HAV Hydrogen, which is part of Euronext Growth Oslo-listed HAV Group ASA.
**Hydrogen supply**
Leading hydrogen producer and distributor Norwegian Hydrogen will provide security of supply for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns by providing all the green hydrogen fuel for the five dry bulk carriers.
“We are proud to partner with Maris Fiducia and HAV Hydrogen on this groundbreaking project, where we will ensure the supply of green hydrogen for all the new vessels. This collaboration highlights the practical steps we are taking to decarbonize the maritime sector, and our joint efforts in this partnership will lead to significant emission reductions for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns. We also look forward to seeing the positive impact these hydrogen-powered vessels will have on advancing green shipping technologies”, says Jens Berge, CEO of Norwegian Hydrogen.
Enova supports tomorrow’s energy and climate technology, so that Norway will reach the climate goals for 2050. Enova manages the Climate and Energy Fund and is a state-owned enterprise owned by the Norwegian Ministry of Climate and Energy.
2,957

Press release
# Maris Fiducia teams up with HAV Hydrogen, Norwegian Hydrogen and Ankerbeer for zero emission bulk shipping 
Maris Fiducia Norway has entered into cooperation agreements with maritime technology company HAV Hydrogen and the hydrogen producer and distributor Norwegian Hydrogen with the objective of developing, building and operating hydrogen-powered dry-bulk vessels in Europe. 
![Maris Fiducia](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fcybt4if0%2Fproduction%2F11f7fa160a2fc864b34cbc30ad2105aab649c09a-1920x1080.jpg&w=3840&q=75)
![Maris Fiducia](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fcybt4if0%2Fproduction%2F11f7fa160a2fc864b34cbc30ad2105aab649c09a-1920x1080.jpg&w=750&q=75)
The vessels are designed by the Dutch ship designer Ankerbeer. Upon successful realization of the project, the vessels will go on hire through a zero-emission time charter agreement with Schulte & Bruns, establishing a commercial operation for vessels operating on hydrogen fuel, proving the feasibility of hydrogen technologies for shipping. The vessels shall be classed by DNV and registered in the NIS register.
Maris Fiducia is dedicated to shaping the future of maritime transportation towards environmental sustainability and our chartering partners Schulte & Bruns share this vision. Using hydrogen as fuel is a natural step in this development and the versatility of HAV Hydrogen’s ZEPOD, coupled with Norwegian Hydrogen’s hydrogen infrastructure, represent what is required to realise such ambitions. 
Markku Vedder, CEO of the Maris Fiducia Group
The group operates a fleet of over twenty dry cargo and tanker vessels, and its Norwegian subsidiary Maris Fiducia Norway AS shall operate the hydrogen-powered vessels. The group’s primary focus is environmentally friendly maritime transportation logistics for reputable customers. The company has decades of experience with the development of advanced ship design and solutions for sustainable shipping with a track-record of over 100 newbuilds.
HAV Hydrogen brings their specialist competence in the development of complete and scalable hydrogen-based energy systems to the partnership. The company has developed and obtained Approval in Principle from DNV for its Zero Emission Pod – ZEPOD® – a deckhouse containing a complete hydrogen energy system for ships. Installed power from a ZEPOD® can be used for main propulsion systems or for auxiliary power onboard a vessel. Subject to potential innovation funding, Maris Fiducia’s chosen shipyard will order ZEPODs from HAV Hydrogen for use on board the vessels.
As a specialist supplier and integrator of maritime fuel cell solutions, there is obviously a limit to how much we can influence the maritime industry on our own. By collaborating with a shipowner and a producer of green hydrogen, we enable a value chain approach that can tear down further barriers towards realisation of hydrogen as maritime fuel.
Kristian Osnes, managing director of HAV Hydrogen, which is part of Euronext Growth Oslo-listed HAV Group ASA
Norwegian Hydrogen is building an array of hydrogen production sites throughout the Nordics and will operate a comprehensive distribution network for green hydrogen. This capability shall provide security of supply for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns.
The cooperation with Maris Fiducia and their founder Markku Vedder, proves there are shipowners with the vision and capability to develop zero-emission shipping solutions. We look forward to the journey onwards together with Maris Fiducia, establishing corridors for sustainable shipping throughout Europe. 
Jens Berge, CEO of Norwegian Hydrogen
### **About Maris Fiducia**
Maris Fiducia, a family-owned company, was established in 2007 and aims to be an innovative leader and best-in-class supplier of environmentally friendly maritime transport solutions. Through its subsidiaries, the group operates a quality fleet of well-maintained tankers and dry cargo ships which are in daily operation. The group has a clear strategy towards environmentally friendly sea transport and logistics solutions for well-known customers, and transport of sustainable products to different industry sectors. The shipping company operates primarily in the North Sea basin, the Baltics and SECA areas (Sulphur Emission Control Area), but also has missions over longer distances and intercontinentally. In addition to Maris Fiducia Norway, the other group companies Grona Ship Management, South End Tanker Management (Wet Cargo) and Q-Shipping (Dry Cargo) support daily operations and the newbuilding programs.
### **About HAV Hydrogen**
HAV Hydrogen delivers complete and scalable hydrogen systems for use on board large and small vessels, newbuilds and retrofitted. Based on our Norwegian heritage, experience, quality focus and innovative solutions, our insight provides our customers with a head start, increases their competitiveness, and enables them to realize the green shift towards a sustainable future at sea.
[**www.havhydrogen.no**](https://www.havhydrogen.no)

The award marks an important step toward realizing the Nordic region’s first industrial-scale liquid hydrogen production facility in Rjukan, Norway. Read more ](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/news/norwegian-hydrogen-awards-feed-contract-to-worley-for-rjukanlh2-project)
[![MaserFrakt truck](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fcybt4if0%2Fproduction%2F344ac6fa50455d2c018ce4664399c69d45d82fa4-3000x1996.jpg%3Frect%3D3%2C0%2C2994%2C1996%26w%3D420%26h%3D280&w=1080&q=75) 26/02/2026 MaserFrakt and Norwegian Hydrogen sign long-term contract for the supply of green hydrogen MaserFrakt, one of Sweden’s largest providers of heavy-duty transport, construction machinery, and logistics services has partnered with Norwegian Hydrogen for the supply of green hydrogen for MaserFrakt’s vehicle operations. The collaboration is an important step in both parties' ambition to contribute to the green transition, enabling zero emissions in heavy transport. Read more ](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/news/maserfrakt-and-norwegian-hydrogen-sign-long-term-contract-for-the-supply-of-green-hydrogen)
[![Ólafur Orri Ólafsson CEO of Samskip \(left\), Jens Berge CEO Norwegian Hydrogen](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fcybt4if0%2Fproduction%2Ffc6147692cf858649938b6ae6b39cd2504430e7e-3829x2957.jpg%3Frect%3D0%2C203%2C3829%2C2553%26w%3D420%26h%3D280&w=1080&q=75) 07/01/2026 Samskip moves forward with Norwegian Hydrogen as its preferred supplier of liquid green hydrogen European logistics company and shipowner Samskip has selected Norwegian Hydrogen as preferred supplier for liquid green hydrogen (LH2) for the two SeaShuttle container vessels it has currently under construction. A Memorandum of Understanding for further cooperation was signed by the parties on Friday 5 December 2025. Read more ](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/news/samskip-moves-forward-with-norwegian-hydrogen-as-its-preferred-supplier-of-liquid-green-hydrogen)
[![Norwegian Hydrogen - illustration at Rjukan](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fcybt4if0%2Fproduction%2F5ca5c48546321ecc65e74560ca213b7f55c4c3b0-2000x1125.png%3Frect%3D156%2C0%2C1688%2C1125%26w%3D420%26h%3D280&w=1080&q=75) 03/11/2025 More support for Rjukan liquid hydrogen project with EUR 31.5 million grant from EU Innovation Fund  Norwegian Hydrogen AS has been offered a EUR 31.5 million grant from the EU Innovation Fund for its pioneering RjukanLH2 project, which will establish Norway’s first complete value chain for green liquid hydrogen (LH2) dedicated to maritime transport. Read more ](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/news/more-support-for-rjukan-liquid-hydrogen-project-with-eur-31-5-million-grant-from-eu-innovation)
[![Green Iron](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Fcybt4if0%2Fproduction%2F37d868011717b5fbc2cb9420dcf5870fed23dfea-1200x1200.png%3Frect%3D0%2C200%2C1200%2C800%26w%3D420%26h%3D280&w=1080&q=75) 19/08/2025 Norwegian Hydrogen and GreenIron sign strategic agreement to scale fossil-free metal production In October last year, Norwegian Hydrogen AS and GreenIron H2 AB announced their agreement for delivery of green hydrogen from Norwegian Hydrogen’s production plant in Hellesylt, Norway, to GreenIron’s first full-scale furnace for direct reduction of metals in Sandviken, Sweden. Now, the two companies have broadened their collaboration, signing a strategic agreement to co-locate and build green hydrogen production plants and direct reduction furnaces at additional locations. Read more ](https://norwegianhydrogen.com/news/norwegian-hydrogen-and-greeniron-sign-strategic-agreement-to-scale-fossil-free-metal-production)

# Aleksander Berentsen 


30 July 2021

    
WORKING PAPER SERIES - No. 2578
[A unified framework for CBDC design: remuneration, collateral haircuts and quantity constraints](https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/pdf/scpwps/ecb.wp2578~bd5e2c4fdf.en.pdf?198968c8f25ca156d741f8a8609e00f0)
  * [Katrin Assenmacher](https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/research/authors/profiles/katrin-assenmacher.en.html)
  * [Aleksander Berentsen](https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/research/authors/profiles/aleksander-berentsen.en.html)
  * [Claus Brand](https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/research/authors/profiles/claus-brand.en.html)
  * [Nora Lamersdorf](https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/research/authors/profiles/nora-lamersdorf.en.html)


[ English](https://www.ecb.europa.eu/pub/pdf/scpwps/ecb.wp2578~bd5e2c4fdf.en.pdf)
Details 

Abstract
    We study the macroeconomic effects of central bank digital currency (CBDC) in a dynamic general equilibrium model. Timing and information frictions create a need for inside (bank deposits) and outside money (CBDC) to finance production. To steer the quantity of CBDC, the central bank can set the lending and deposit rates for CBDC as well as collateral and quantity requirements. Less restrictive provision of CBDC reduces bank deposits. A positive interest spread on CBDC or stricter collateral or quantity constraints reduce welfare but can contain bank disintermediation, especially if the elasticity of substitution between bank deposits and CBDC is small.

**E41** : Macroeconomics and Monetary Economics→Money and Interest Rates→Demand for Money  
**E42** : Macroeconomics and Monetary Economics→Money and Interest Rates→Monetary Systems, Standards, Regimes, Government and the Monetary System, Payment Systems  
**E51** : Macroeconomics and Monetary Economics→Monetary Policy, Central Banking, and the Supply of Money and Credit→Money Supply, Credit, Money Multipliers  
**E52** : Macroeconomics and Monetary Economics→Monetary Policy, Central Banking, and the Supply of Money and Credit→Monetary Policy
